slides.title: Buod ng Ikatlong Linggo
slides.text: Binabati kita! Natapos mo ang lahat ng slide sa linggong ito. Natutunan natin ang kahulugan ng pag-iimpok at pagtitipid. Nalaman natin ang pagtatabi ng barya sa alkansyang kawayan. Natutunan natin ang pagtitipid sa pagkain, tubig, at ilaw. Iniwasan natin ang pagsasayang ng yaman at maling pagbili. Gumawa tayo ng plano ng pagtitipid kasama ang pamilya. Handa ka na ba sa pagsusulit at hamon ng linggong ito? Ipagpatuloy ang pagiging matipid at disiplinadong bata!

examples.title: Tandaan
examples.content: Pag-iimpok = Pera para sa bukas. Pagtitipid = Matalinong paggamit. Tipirin ang yaman!

standard.q: Ano ang tawag sa pagtatabi ng labis na barya o pera para sa hinaharap?
Ano ang tawag sa matalino at maingat na paggamit ng mga likas na yaman?
Saan karaniwang inilalagay ng mga bata ang kanilang naimpok na barya sa bahay?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang nagpapakita ng pagtitipid sa kuryente?
options: hayaang nakabukas ang bentilador kapag walang tao
patayin ang ilaw at bentilador kapag lalabas ng silid-aralan
gamitin ang lamp kahit maliwanag ang araw

standard.q: Paano mo maiiwasan ang pagsasayang ng pagkain sa inyong hapag-kainan?
options: kumuha ng napakaraming pagkain at magtira sa plato
kumuha lamang ng sapat na sukat at ubusin ang nasa plato

standard.q: Ano ang dapat gawin sa gripo habang nagsesepilyo ng ngipin sa banyo?
options: hayaang nakabukas at tumutulo ang tubig
isara ang gripo at gumamit ng baso

standard.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang nagpapakita ng pagtitipid sa mga gamit sa eskwela?
options: punitin ang mga blankong papel sa notebook
gamitin ang lapis hanggang sa ito ay maging munti nang may pag-iingat
itapon ang krayola kapag nabali ang dulo

standard.q: Sino ang makikinabang kapag ang buong pamilya ay nagtutulungan sa pag-iimpok at pagtitipid?
options: ang mga pulis sa outpost lamang

standard.q: Ano ang angkop na gamitin sa paliligo upang makatipid sa tubig kumpara sa shower?
options: gripo na walang tigil ang tulo

standard.q: Tama o Mali: Ang pagtitipid ng kuryente sa pamamagitan ng pagpatay ng ilaw ay nakatutulong sa badyet ng pamilya.
Ano ang dapat gawin sa mga tirang pagkain na maaari pang kainin sa susunod na kainan?
options: itabi nang maayos sa refrigerator upang maiinit muli
iwanan sa mesa nang walang takip

standard.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pambalana sa pook kung saan tayo nag-iimpok ng pera sa komunidad?
Anong materyal sa kalikasan ang karaniwang ginagamit sa paggawa ng katutubong alkansya?
Punan ang patlang: 'Ang bawat ___ ng naimpok na barya ay may halaga para sa hinaharap.'
Ano ang dapat mong gawin kapag nais mong bumili ng bagong laruan ngunit may laruan ka pa sa bahay?
options: umiyak sa harap ng nanay upang ibili ka
magtiyaga sa lumang laruan at iimpok ang barya sa alkansya

standard.q: Saan maaaring gamitin ang pinaghugasan ng bigas sa bahay upang makatipid sa tubig?
options: ipangdilig sa mga halaman sa hardin

standard.q: Tama o Mali: Ang pagbili ng mga mamahaling gamit na hindi kailangan sa paaralan ay bahagi ng pagtitipid.
Aling ugali ang ipinapakita ng batang nagsusulat sa likod ng mga lumang papel na malinis pa?
Ano ang angkop na sabihin sa kapatid na hinayaang nakabukas ang TV kahit walang nanonood?
options: 'Patayin natin ang telebisyon upang makatipid tayo sa kuryente.'
'Ilipat natin sa cartoon kahit matutulog na tayo.'

standard.q: Ano ang pangunahing benepisyo ng pag-iimpok para sa isang Grade 3 student?
options: makabili ng pinakamataas na tatak ng sapatos
makabili ng sariling gamit sa eskwela sa pasukan nang hindi umaasa sa magulang

standard.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang tamang kilos kapag nakita mong umaapaw ang tubig sa drum ng barangay?
options: isara ang gripo nang kusa upang hindi masayang ang tubig
huwag pansinin dahil hindi mo naman drum ito

standard.q: Tukuyin ang pambalana sa bagay na nagbibigay-liwanag sa desk na ginagamitan ng kuryente:
Tama o Mali: Ang pagkusa sa pagpatay ng ilaw kapag umaalis sa silid ay nagpapakita ng disiplina sa sarili.
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang nagpapakita ng tamang pagtitipid sa pagkain sa paaralan?
options: magbaon ng napakaraming pagkain at itapon ang hindi naubos
magbaon ng sapat na pagkain at ubusin ito sa oras ng recess
bumili ng junk food sa labas ng gate

standard.q: Saan dapat magsimula ang pagtuturo ng pagtitipid at pag-iimpok?
options: sa loob ng tahanan kasama ang pamilya araw-araw
kapag malaki na at nagtatrabaho na

challenge.q: Challenge: Kung ang isang pamilya ay patuloy na nagsasayang ng kuryente at tubig, ano ang maaaring mangyari sa kanilang badyet?
options: Lalaki ang kanilang babayarang bill na magdudulot ng kakulangan sa pera para sa pagkain at iba pang pangangailangan.
Mababawasan ang kanilang bayarin sa bangko.
Mas magiging masaya ang Mayor ng bayan.
Walang magiging epekto ito sa pamilya.

challenge.q: Challenge: Paano nakatutulong ang pag-iimpok ng barya sa alkansya sa paghahanda para sa mga hindi inaasahang pangyayari (emergencies)?
options: Ang naimpok na pera ay magagamit upang ibili ng gamot o kagamitan sa oras ng biglaang pangangailangan nang hindi nangungutang.
Ang pera ay mabubulok sa loob ng kawayan.
Hindi na kailangang magtrabaho ng mga magulang.
Maaaring ipamigay ang pera sa mga kalaro sa plaza.

challenge.q: Challenge: Aling paraan ang pinakamahusay upang makatipid sa pagbili ng mga aklat sa paaralan kung may kapatid kang katatapos lang sa Grade 3?
options: bumili ng bago at mamahaling aklat sa mall
gamitin at ingatan ang mga lumang aklat ng kapatid na maaari pang basahin
huwag nang mag-aklat kailanman sa klase
punitin ang mga aklat ng kapatid

challenge.q: Challenge: Bakit ang paggamit ng pinaghugasan ng bigas o gulay sa pagdidilig ng halaman ay itinuturing na matalinong pagtitipid?
options: Dahil nakakatipid ito sa paggamit ng malinis na tubig mula sa gripo sa pamamagitan ng pag-recycle ng tubig.
Dahil mas gusto ng halaman ang tubig-bigas kaysa sa ulan.
Dahil mas madali itong gawin kaysa sa paglilinis ng pader.
Dahil binabayaran ng barangay ang pamilya sa pag-recycle.

challenge.q: Challenge: Aling ugnayan ang pinakatumpak sa pagitan ng disiplina sa pag-iimpok at pagmamahal sa magulang?
options: Ang matipid na bata ay nagpapagaan sa pinansyal na gastusin ng kanyang mga magulang na nagpapakita ng pagmamahal at pag-aalaga sa pamilya.
Walang ugnayan ang pagtitipid ng anak sa pagmamahal sa magulang.
Nag-iimpok ang bata upang bigyan ng laruan ang kanyang sarili lamang.
Nagtitipid ang bata upang hindi na siya pakainin ng magulang.

performance.title: Aking Plano ng Pagtitipid sa Bahay
performance.desc: Gumawa ng isang simpleng listahan o tsart ng pagtitipid sa inyong bahay. Isulat ang tatlong paraan kung paano mo titipirin ang tubig at kuryente sa inyong tahanan (hal. pagpatay ng ilaw kapag walang tao, paggamit ng baso sa pagse-sepilyo). Ipakita ang tsart na ito sa iyong magulang at talakayin kung paano ito makatutulong sa badyet ng pamilya.
labels: Wastong natukoy ang tatlong paraan ng pagtitipid sa bahay
Maayos na naisulat at naiguhit ang tsart ng pagtitipid sa papel
Malinaw na naipaliwanag ang badyet at plano ng pagtitipid sa magulang

pages:
Pagsasanay 1: Pag-iimpok vs. Pagtitipid

Tukuyin kung ang sitwasyon ay nagpapakita ng Pag-iimpok (saving money) o Pagtitipid (conserving resources) gamit ang stylus.

1. Nagtabi ng limang piso si Matteo mula sa kanyang baon sa kanyang alkansya.
Pag-iimpok
2. Isinara ni Lani ang gripo habang sinasabon ang kanyang mga kamay.
Pagtitipid
3. Pinatay ni Kiko ang bentilador bago lumabas ng silid-aralan.
Pagtitipid

Pagsasanay 2: Matalinong Paggamit ng Yaman

Isulat kung ang gawi sa bahay ay Matipid (karapat-dapat) o Matalak (dapat iwasan/sinasayang ang yaman).

1. Paggamit ng pinaghugasan ng bigas sa pagdidilig ng halaman.
Matipid
2. Hayaang nakasaksak ang TV kahit natutulog na ang lahat.
Matalak
3. Kumuha ng sapat na pagkain lamang at ubusin ang nasa plato.
Matipid

Pagsasanay 3: Pagpaplano ng Pag-iimpok

Sagutin ang tanong gamit ang tumpak na detalye batay sa iyong sariling alkansya at aralin.

Bakit mahalagang mag-impok ng barya ang isang bata sa kanyang alkansya araw-araw?
Upang may magamit na sariling pera sa oras ng pangangailangan o pambili ng gamit sa eskwela.
Nakatutulong din ito upang matutong maging disiplinado at matipid sa paglipas ng panahon.

slides.title: Story: The Honest Woodcutter - Part 1
slides.text: Once upon a time, a poor woodcutter lived in a small cottage near a deep forest. He worked hard every day, cutting down old trees for firewood to feed his family. One afternoon, as he was chopping a tree branch near the bank of a deep, fast-flowing river, his iron axe slipped from his sweaty hands and splashed into the water. The woodcutter sat by the muddy bank and began to weep. He was very sad because the river was deep, and he could not swim. The iron axe was his only tool, and he could not afford to buy a new one. Without his axe, he could not earn any money to buy food. Suddenly, a beautiful water spirit rose up from the bubbles. The woodcutter was very poor but worked hard every day to support his family. He walked to the forest early in the morning and spent hours cutting wood. When his axe fell into the deep river, he felt helpless because he did not have money to buy a new one, and without it, he could not earn a living.

slides.title: Story: The Honest Woodcutter - Part 2
slides.text: The water spirit saw his tears and asked, 'Why are you crying, my friend?' The woodcutter explained his trouble. Hearing this, the spirit dove down into the deep water and returned holding a shining golden axe. 'Is this yours?' she asked. 'No,' replied the honest woodcutter. 'That is not my axe.' The spirit dove again and returned with a beautiful silver axe. 'Is this yours?' she asked. 'No,' he said. Finally, she dove a third time and brought up his old, rusty iron axe. 'Yes! That is mine!' he cried happily. The spirit smiled. She was so pleased with his honesty that she decided to reward him. 'Take all three axes,' she said, placing them in his hands. The woodcutter was surprised to see a beautiful water fairy rise from the river, holding a shining golden axe. The fairy asked if the gold axe was his, but the honest woodcutter immediately said no. He did not let the sight of wealth tempt him, choosing to tell the truth instead of taking something that was not his.

slides.title: Story: The Honest Woodcutter - Part 3
slides.text: The woodcutter thanked the spirit and returned to his village with the gold, silver, and iron axes. He was now rich, but he remained a humble and kind man. A greedy neighbor heard his story and wanted to get rich too. He went to the river bank, threw his iron axe into the water, and began crying. The water spirit rose up and dove down, bringing a golden axe. 'Is this yours?' she asked. 'Yes, yes! That is mine!' lied the greedy neighbor, reaching for it. The spirit was angry at his lie. She took the golden axe and disappeared under the water, leaving the greedy neighbor with nothing, not even his own iron axe. He walked home empty-handed and sad. The fairy was so pleased with the woodcutter's honesty that she gave him all three axes as a reward. The woodcutter returned home happily and shared the good news with his family. He proved that honesty is always the best policy, and that being truthful brings respect, peace of mind, and unexpected rewards in the end.

slides.title: Vocabulary & Word Study
slides.text:
Vocabulary:
• Woodcutter - A person who cuts down trees for firewood.
• Wept - Cried past tense.
• Afford - Have enough money to pay for something.

Observe how the prefix 'wood-' and suffix '-cutter' combine to form a new noun!

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. Why did the woodcutter cry?
2. What three axes did the water spirit bring up?
3. Why did the water spirit reward him with all three axes?

slides.title: Moral Lesson & Reflection
slides.text:
Moral Focus: Honesty is the best policy.

The spirit was so pleased with the woodcutter's honesty that she gave him all three axes as a reward. Honesty brings respect and true rewards, whereas greed can lose us even what we have.

Discuss: When is a time you chose to tell the truth?

slides.title: Poem: The Whispering Wind - Part 1
slides.text:
I hear the wind go whispering by,
Under the blue and open sky.
It tells a tale of distant trees,
And dancing leaves and busy bees.

When I walk outside in the afternoon, I can feel a gentle breeze brushing against my cheeks. The wind makes a soft whistling sound as it moves through the green leaves of the big mango trees. It seems to carry stories from far-away places that I have never visited. The leaves dance and shake, and the colorful flowers nod their heads as if they are listening to the wind's secrets. It is a peaceful, beautiful sight that makes me feel happy and relaxed. I stand still and listen closely to the whispering sounds of nature around me, enjoying the calm and quiet day. The wind's whispers are a gentle reminder of the beauty and life that surrounds us in nature. It travels over mountains and seas, carrying seeds and helping plants reproduce. It also helps cool the hot earth, bringing comfort to animals and people. We can learn a lot by listening quietly to the sounds of nature.

slides.title: Poem: The Whispering Wind - Part 2
slides.text:
It travels far, it travels wide,
With secrets locked up deep inside.
It has no hands, it has no feet,
Yet sings a song so soft and sweet.

The wind is a mysterious traveler. It can cross high mountains, fly over deep oceans, and run through busy city streets without ever stopping. It has no physical body, so we cannot see it with our eyes, but we can see the results of its work. It pushes sailboats across the water and lifts colorful kites high into the air. When it is gentle, it sings a sweet song, rustling the grass. But when it is strong, it can roar like a lion. The wind is a powerful force of nature that connects all parts of the world. It is always moving, bringing changes to the weather. The wind also plays with the colorful kites that children fly in the park, lifting them high into the blue sky. It fills the sails of boats, helping them travel across the lakes and oceans. The wind is a powerful but invisible force of nature that helps us in many different ways every day.

slides.title: Poem: The Whispering Wind - Part 3
slides.text:
It cools the earth on hot summer days,
And pushes clouds in gentle ways.
So listen close when breezes blow,
And learn the secrets winds may know.

During hot afternoons, the cool breeze is a wonderful gift. It helps lower the temperature and makes us feel refreshed. The wind also plays a major role in our weather, pushing clouds across the sky to bring rain to thirsty crops. Scientists study the wind's speed and direction to predict the weather. By observing how the wind moves, we learn about physics and earth science. So next time you feel a breeze, close your eyes and listen to the whispering wind, appreciating the natural wonders of our environment. It has many lessons to teach us if we pay attention. The wind's cycle is a natural part of our weather system, bringing rain clouds to water the fields and clean the air. It works together with the sun and water to keep our planet green and healthy. By observing the wind, we can understand the science of weather and appreciate the delicate balance of our environment.

slides.title: Rhyme Scheme & Phonics
slides.text:
Phonics Focus: Rhyming Words
Let's identify the rhyming pairs in the poem:
• by / sky
• trees / bees
• wide / inside
• feet / sweet

Rhymes help create a natural rhythm in poems.

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. What color is the sky in the poem?
2. Name two things the wind tells tales about.
3. What secrets does the wind carry?

slides.title: Moral Focus: Observation
slides.text:
Nature is full of quiet beauty. By slowing down and listening to the wind or observing the leaves, we learn to appreciate the world around us.

Discuss: Close your eyes for one minute. What sounds of nature can you hear around you right now?

slides.title: Tongue Twister Fun - Part 1
slides.text: Welcome to our final speech gym challenge! Today we will visit a beautiful sandy seashore where a girl named Sally sells shells. Sally sells seashells by the seashore. The shells she sells are surely seashells. So if she sells shells on the seashore, I'm sure she sells seashore shells. Let's try saying this together: 'She sells seashells by the seashore.' Make sure you make a clean distinction between the soft /s/ sound in 'sells' and the soft /sh/ sound in 'shells'. Say it three times, starting slowly and then speeding up! It is a great exercise to train your tongue and lips for clear pronunciation and enunciation. Speak clearly to show your confidence. To make a clean sound, practice repeating these words slowly with your classmates. Sally worked hard to collect the most beautiful shells, placing them carefully in her basket. She wanted to show them to her friends and teach them about the different types of sea creatures that live in the ocean, sharing her knowledge.

slides.title: Tongue Twister Fun - Part 2
slides.text: Next, let's meet our old friend Peter Piper again. He is picking peppers in the garden. Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers. Did Peter Piper pick a peck of pickled peppers? If Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers, where's the peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked? A peck is a unit of dry measure, about two gallons! That is a lot of peppers. Let's practice saying the 'P' sounds: 'Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.' Make sure your lips pop clearly for each 'P' sound. Try saying it three times rapidly without stumbling or mixing up the words. Polly the puppy watched Peter and barked happily at him. Practicing these tongue twisters is a fun way to improve our pronunciation and speaking skills. It helps us speak clearly when we read books, ask questions in class, or tell stories to our family. Remember to focus on the different sounds, letting your tongue and lips move smoothly and accurately as you practice.

slides.title: Tongue Twister Fun - Part 3
slides.text: Finally, let's try a new twister about silly sheep sleeping in a shed. Six silly sheep slept soundly in the wooden shed. The sheep should sleep in the shed, not in the grass. Let's say: 'Six silly sheep slept soundly in the shed.' Say it as fast as you can! If you make a mistake, don't worry. Just take a deep breath, slow down, and try again. Practicing these tongue twisters daily helps improve your reading speed, speech clarity, and verbal confidence. Announcers, actors, and teachers practice them to speak clearly. You are doing a wonderful job. Keep up the good work and have fun with speech! Practice makes perfect. Speech exercises also help us build confidence when speaking in front of others. By practicing regularly, we can overcome our speaking difficulties and express our ideas clearly and effectively. Don't be afraid to make mistakes; just slow down, breathe, and try again, showing determination and patience as you learn. Clear speaking is a superpower that helps us communicate better.

slides.title: Speech & Pronunciation Practice
slides.text:
Phonics Focus: S-blend / Sh-sound contrast

Tongue twisters help train our vocal cords and tongue muscles to enunciate clearly. Practice saying 'sells' (soft /s/) and 'shells' (soft /sh/) back-to-back.

slides.title: Comprehension & Speech Tasks
slides.text:
Practice Activities:
1. Say Twister 1 three times, slowly increasing your speed.
2. Say Twister 2 without stumbling on the 'P' sound.
3. What does 'peck' mean? (A unit of dry measure).

slides.title: Focus: Perseverance
slides.text:
Learning to speak clearly takes practice. Don't worry if you slip up; tongue twisters are meant to be challenging! Keep trying and have fun with it.

Discuss: Why is it important to speak slowly and clearly when giving a speech?

thursday.title: Dr. Jose Rizal: The Avid Reader

slides.title: Biography: Dr. Jose Rizal - Part 1
slides.text: Dr. Jose Rizal is the national hero of the Philippines, and he was an exceptionally smart and curious boy. When he was young, his family called him Pepe. Pepe lived in Calamba, Laguna, and he loved to read books more than playing outside. Back then, books were rare and expensive. Pepe's mother, Teodora Alonso, was his very first teacher. She taught him how to read the alphabet and read him stories every night under the warm light of an oil lamp. Pepe listened carefully to every word, fascinated by the stories of distant places and heroes. He realized that reading was the key to understanding the world. He read every single book he could find. Jose's mother was his first teacher, and she taught him how to read the alphabet and pray. She noticed his love for books and encouraged him to read stories that taught moral lessons. Jose spent hours reading, letting his imagination travel to distant lands and learning about different cultures and ideas from around the world.

slides.title: Biography: Dr. Jose Rizal - Part 2
slides.text: One quiet evening, Pepe's mother read him a famous fable about a young moth. The young moth was fascinated by the bright, glowing flame of a candle. The mother moth warned her child: 'Do not fly too close to the flame, my child, or your wings will burn!' But the young moth did not listen. It was attracted to the light, flew too close, and its wings burned, causing it to fall into the hot oil. Pepe sat in silence, watching the real moths fly around their oil lamp. He never forgot this story. He learned that we must listen to the wise advice of our parents and teachers to keep ourselves safe from danger. He was a good boy. Jose was a very bright student who loved to learn languages, science, and history. He believed that education was the key to helping his country and improving the lives of his people. He studied hard in different schools and universities, always seeking to gain new knowledge and skills that he could use to help others.

slides.title: Biography: Dr. Jose Rizal - Part 3
slides.text: As Pepe grew up, his love for reading and learning never stopped. He went to school in Manila and later traveled to Europe to study medicine, becoming a skilled eye doctor. He also learned to speak over twenty languages! He wrote two famous novels, 'Noli Me Tangere' and 'El Filibusterismo', which helped show the difficulties of the Filipino people and inspired them to seek independence. He became a great artist, writer, and hero. Jose Rizal proved that reading books can open up the entire world to us and give us the power to help our country. He is an inspiration for all Filipino children to study hard. We honor him today. Jose Rizal's books and writings inspired the Filipino people to unite and fight for their freedom and rights. He remains a national hero because of his patriotism, intelligence, and dedication to education. His story teaches us that reading and learning are powerful tools that can change our lives and help our country succeed.

slides.title: Vocabulary & Word Study
slides.text:
Vocabulary:
• National Hero - A person recognized for their exceptional contribution to their country.
• Attracted - Drawn or pulled towards something.
• Advice - Recommendations or guidance offered.

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. Who was Pepe's first teacher?
2. What animal was the main character in the story Pepe's mother read?
3. What did Pepe learn from the story of the moth?

slides.title: Focus: Lifelong Learning
slides.text:
Dr. Jose Rizal became a doctor, a writer, an artist, and a hero because he never stopped reading and learning. Books open up the entire world to us.

Discuss: Like Pepe, what is a story your parents or teachers told you that you still remember?

slides.title: Story: The Boy Who Cried Wolf - Part 1
slides.text: Once, a young shepherd boy was given the duty of tending a flock of sheep on a grassy hill near a quiet forest. The job was lonely, and the boy got very bored sitting on the hill all day with nothing to do. To amuse himself, he decided to play a trick on the villagers. He ran down towards the village, waving his arms and shouting at the top of his lungs, 'Wolf! Wolf! A wolf is chasing the sheep!' The villagers stopped their work, grabbed sticks and axes, and rushed up the hill to help him. When they arrived, they found the sheep grazing peacefully, and the boy laughed at them. The villagers were very angry at him. The boy felt very lonely and bored on the hillside, watching the sheep eat grass all day. He wanted some excitement and attention, so he decided to play a trick on the villagers. He did not think about the consequences of his actions, choosing to tell a lie just to see the people run to help.

slides.title: Story: The Boy Who Cried Wolf - Part 2
slides.text: The villagers warned the boy, 'Do not cry wolf when there is no wolf!' They went back to their work. However, a few days later, the shepherd boy got bored again. He decided to play the same trick. He ran down the hill screaming, 'Wolf! Wolf!' The villagers rushed up the hill again. Once more, they found no wolf, only the boy laughing at their worried faces. 'A liar will not be believed, even when he tells the truth!' warned the wise village elder. The boy just ignored the warning and kept laughing. Then, one afternoon, a real, hungry wolf came out from the dark forest and attacked the sheep. The boy was very scared. The boy laughed at the angry villagers, thinking it was a very funny joke. He did not realize that by telling lies, he was losing their trust and respect. The villagers warned him that lying is wrong and that nobody believes a liar, even when he tells the truth. But the boy did not listen to their advice.

slides.title: Story: The Boy Who Cried Wolf - Part 3
slides.text: The boy was terrified. He ran towards the village, screaming louder than ever, 'Help! Wolf! Wolf! A real wolf is eating the sheep! Please help!' The villagers heard his cries, but they smiled and said to each other, 'He is just playing another trick to laugh at us. We will not go.' Nobody went up the hill to help him. The wolf killed many sheep and scattered the flock into the forest. The boy sat on the hill and cried, realizing his lies had cost him his flock. He learned that when we lie, we destroy the trust of others. Once trust is broken, people will not believe us even when we tell the truth. Honesty is very important. The boy shivered in fear as the wolf came closer, but his cries were ignored by the villagers. He realized too late that his lies had cost him the safety of his sheep and the trust of his community. He learned that honesty is essential, and that we must always tell the truth to keep our friends' trust.

slides.title: Vocabulary & Word Study
slides.text:
Vocabulary:
• Shepherd - A person who guards and tends sheep.
• Tending - Caring for or watching over.
• Amuse - Entertain or cause amusement.

Observe how 'shepherd' combines 'sheep' and 'herd'!

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. Why did the boy cry 'Wolf' when there was no wolf?
2. Why didn't the villagers come the third time?
3. What happened to the sheep at the end?

slides.title: Moral Lesson & Reflection
slides.text:
Moral Focus: Trustworthiness.

A liar will not be believed, even when they speak the truth. Trust takes a long time to build but can be destroyed in a single moment.

Discuss: Why is it important for people to be able to trust your word?

slides.title: Kuwento: Ang Langgam at ang Tipaklong - Bahagi 1
slides.text: Noong panahon ng tag-araw, ang buong kagubatan ay napakaganda at puno ng pagkain. Habang si Tipaklong ay masayang kumakanta at sumasayaw sa ilalim ng init ng araw, si Langgam naman ay abalang-abala sa paghahanap at paghahakot ng mga butil ng palay at prutas patungo sa kanyang munting bahay. 'Langgam, halika muna rito at magsaya tayo! Napakaraming pagkain ngayon, bakit ka nagpapapagod?' tanong ni Tipaklong habang tumutugtog ng gitara. Sumagot si Langgam, 'Nag-iipon ako ng pagkain para sa darating na tag-ulan upang hindi kami magutom. Dapat mag-ipon ka na rin, Tipaklong.' Ngunit tinawanan lamang siya ni Tipaklong at nagpatuloy sa paglalaro.

slides.title: Kuwento: Ang Langgam at ang Tipaklong - Bahagi 2
slides.text: Mabilis na lumipas ang mga araw at dumating ang panahon ng tag-ulan. Ulan dito, ulan doon, at naging napakalamig ng buong kagubatan. Nabasa ang mga dahon at walang makitang pagkain sa paligid dahil nalubog sa tubig ang mga damo. Ligtas na nanatili si Langgam sa loob ng kanyang tuyo at mainit na tahanan, kasama ang kanyang pamilya habang masayang kumakain ng mga butil na naipon nila noong tag-araw. Samantala, si Tipaklong ay gutom na gutom at nanginginig sa lamig sa labas, walang makitang masisilungan at walang makain.

slides.title: Kuwento: Ang Langgam at ang Tipaklong - Bahagi 3
slides.text: Dahil sa matinding gutom, lumapit si Tipaklong sa bahay ni Langgam at kumatok. 'Kaibigang Langgam, pakiusap at bigyan mo ako kahit kaunting pagkain. Nanginginig na ako sa gutom,' pakiusap niya. Nakaramdam ng awa si Langgam, kaya pinapasok niya si Tipaklong at binigyan ng mainit na pagkain at tuyong dahon. 'Salamat, Langgam. Ngayon ay naintindihan ko na kung bakit ka nagtrabaho noong tag-araw habang ako ay naglaro lamang,' wika ni Tipaklong nang may pagsisisi. Nangako si Tipaklong na sa susunod na tag-araw, mag-iipon din siya ng pagkain tulad ng kanyang masipag na kaibigan.

slides.title: Talasalitaan at Pag-aaral ng Salita
slides.text: Mga Bokabularyo: • Tag-araw - Panahon ng init at sikat ng araw. • Paghahakot - Pagsasama-sama o pagdadala ng mga bagay sa isang lugar. • Pagsisisi - Pakiramdam ng lungkot dahil sa maling desisyong ginawa. Tutok sa Phonics: Magsanay sa pagbigkas ng tunog ng 'l' sa 'langgam', 'lamig', at 'laro'.

slides.title: Mga Tanong sa Pag-unawa
slides.text: Mga Tanong: 1. Ano ang ginagawa ni Langgam habang naglalaro si Tipaklong? 2. Ano ang nangyari kay Tipaklong nang dumating ang tag-ulan? 3. Paano tinulungan ni Langgam si Tipaklong sa dulo ng kwento?

slides.title: Mabuting Aral at Pagninilay
slides.text: Kaisipan: Ang pagiging masipag at handa sa hinaharap ay nagliligtas sa atin sa hirap. May oras para sa paglalaro at may oras para sa pagtatrabaho. Ang pagiging handa ay nagpapakita ng katalinuhan, habang ang pagtulong sa nangangailangan ay nagpapakita ng kabaitan. Talakayin: Ano ang mga aralin sa paaralan na maaari mong pag-aralan nang maaga upang maging handa sa pagsusulit?

slides.title: Kuwento: Si Juan Tamad - Bahagi 1
slides.text: Sa isang maliit na baryo, may isang batang lalaki na nagngangalang Juan. Dahil sa kanyang labis na katamaran, binansagan siya ng kanyang mga kapitbahay na si Juan Tamad. Tuwing inuutusan siya ng kanyang ina, lagi siyang nagdadahilan o gumagawa ng paraan upang hindi matapos ang gawain. Isang mainit na hapon, habang naglalakad si Juan sa tabi ng kalsada, nakakita siya ng isang puno ng bayabas na puno ng malalaki at hinog na bunga. Gustong-gusto niyang kumain ng bayabas, ngunit napakataas ng puno at ayaw niyang magkapagod sa pag-akyat.

slides.title: Kuwento: Si Juan Tamad - Bahagi 2
slides.text: Sa halip na umakyat o kumuha ng mahabang kawayan upang sungkitin ang bayabas, humiga si Juan sa ilalim ng puno. Tumingala siya at tinitigan ang isang napakahinog na bayabas na nakabitin sa mababang sanga. 'Hihiga na lang ako rito at bubuksan ko ang aking bibig. Hihintayin ko na lang na mahulog ang bayabas sa aking bibig,' sabi ni Juan sa sarili. Nanatili siyang nakahiga sa ilalim ng puno ng ilang oras, habang lumilipas ang araw at nagugutom siya. Ang mga ibon naman ay dumapo sa puno at kinain ang ilang bayabas sa itaas habang pinapanood si Juan.

slides.title: Kuwento: Si Juan Tamad - Bahagi 3
slides.text: Nang dumaan ang kanyang ina sa kalsada at nakita si Juan na nakanganga sa ilalim ng puno, napa-iling ito sa lungkot at galit. 'Juan! Bakit ka nakahiga diyan?' tanong ng ina. 'Ina, hinihintay ko pong mahulog ang bayabas sa aking bibig,' sagot ni Juan. Sinabi ng kanyang ina, 'Napakalaking bayabas niyan, Juan! Kung tumayo ka lamang at kinuha ito gamit ang iyong kamay, kanina ka pa busog! Walang pagkain na kusang mahuhulog sa bibig ng taong ayaw gumawa.' Nahiya si Juan at tumayo upang tulungan ang kanyang ina sa pagdadala ng mga kahoy.

slides.title: Talasalitaan at Pag-aaral ng Salita
slides.text: Mga Bokabularyo: • Binansagan - Binigyan ng palayaw o pangalan batay sa ugali. • Sungkitin - Kumuha ng bagay sa itaas gamit ang stick o kawayan. • Kusang - Nangyayari nang walang tulong o kusa ng iba. Tutok sa Phonics: Magsanay sa pagbigkas ng tunog ng 'j' o 'h' sa 'juan', 'hinog', at 'higa'.

slides.title: Mga Tanong sa Pag-unawa
slides.text: Mga Tanong: 1. Bakit binansagan si Juan na 'Juan Tamad'? 2. Paano nakuha ni Juan ang bayabas ayon sa kanyang plano? 3. Ano ang sinabi ng kanyang ina nang makita siyang nakahiga sa ilalim ng puno?

slides.title: Mabuting Aral at Pagninilay
slides.text: Kaisipan: Ang mga pangarap at pangangailangan ay makakamit lamang sa pamamagitan ng pagkilos. Ang katamaran ay nagdadala ng gutom at kawalan ng pag-unlad. Hindi dapat iasa sa tadhana o pagkakataon ang mga bagay na maaari namang pagtrabahuhan. Talakayin: Ano ang mga layunin mo sa buhay na nangangailangan ng iyong pagsisikap upang maabot?

slides.title: Introduction to Lines in Geometry (Advanced)
slides.text:
In geometry, a line is a straight path that extends infinitely in both directions without ending.
We represent lines with arrows on both ends.
Today, we will study how lines relate to each other when they are on the same flat surface.
For instance, consider the example of Line vs Line Segment:
A line segment has two fixed endpoints (like a ruler's edge), while a line extends forever in both directions.
Another case is the study of Naming Lines:
We name a line using two points on it, like line AB, or with a single lowercase letter, like line l.
How does the concept of Introduction to Lines in Geometry (Advanced) help us think critically?

examples.title: Line vs Line Segment
examples.content: A line segment has two fixed endpoints (like a ruler's edge), while a line extends forever in both directions.

examples.title: Naming Lines
examples.content: We name a line using two points on it, like line AB, or with a single lowercase letter, like line l.

slides.title: What are Parallel Lines? (Advanced)
slides.text:
Parallel lines are lines on the same flat surface that are always the same distance apart (equidistant) and never meet or cross each other, no matter how far they extend.
This slide introduces key insights about What are Parallel Lines? (Advanced).
For instance, consider the example of Railway Tracks:
The two rails of a straight train track are parallel. They must never cross, or the train would derail.
Another case is the study of Lined Paper:
The horizontal blue lines on your writing notebook are parallel lines.
How does the concept of What are Parallel Lines? (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Railway Tracks
examples.content: The two rails of a straight train track are parallel. They must never cross, or the train would derail.

examples.title: Lined Paper
examples.content: The horizontal blue lines on your writing notebook are parallel lines.

slides.title: Identifying Parallel Lines (Advanced)
slides.text:
We use the symbol || to show that two lines are parallel.
For example, if line AB is parallel to line CD, we write AB || CD.
They will never intersect.
For instance, consider the example of Opposite Edges:
The top and bottom edges of a rectangular blackboard or tablet screen are parallel.
Another case is the study of Window Grids:
Vertical bars in a window grid are parallel to each other to form a protective cage.
How does the concept of Identifying Parallel Lines (Advanced) help us think critically?

examples.title: Opposite Edges
examples.content: The top and bottom edges of a rectangular blackboard or tablet screen are parallel.

examples.title: Window Grids
examples.content: Vertical bars in a window grid are parallel to each other to form a protective cage.

slides.title: What are Intersecting Lines? (Advanced)
slides.text:
Intersecting lines are lines that cross each other at exactly one point.
The point where they cross is called the point of intersection.
When they cross, they form angles.
For instance, consider the example of Crossroads:
A street intersection where two roads cross each other represents intersecting lines.
Another case is the study of Opened Scissors:
The two blades of an opened pair of scissors form intersecting lines crossing at the central pivot screw.
How does the concept of What are Intersecting Lines? (Advanced) help us think critically?

examples.title: Crossroads
examples.content: A street intersection where two roads cross each other represents intersecting lines.

examples.title: Opened Scissors
examples.content: The two blades of an opened pair of scissors form intersecting lines crossing at the central pivot screw.

slides.title: What are Perpendicular Lines? (Advanced)
slides.text:
Perpendicular lines are a special type of intersecting lines that cross each other to form exactly four right angles (90 degrees).
The symbol for perpendicular is ⊥.
For instance, consider the example of Perfect Cross:
A plus sign (+) or a letter 'T' forms perpendicular lines. The angles formed are exactly 90°.
Another case is the study of Wall and Floor:
A vertical wall meets the flat floor perpendicularly to ensure the building is stable.
How does the concept of What are Perpendicular Lines? (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Perfect Cross
examples.content: A plus sign (+) or a letter 'T' forms perpendicular lines. The angles formed are exactly 90°.

examples.title: Wall and Floor
examples.content: A vertical wall meets the flat floor perpendicularly to ensure the building is stable.

slides.title: Identifying Perpendicular Lines (Advanced)
slides.text:
If line AB is perpendicular to line CD, we write AB ⊥ CD.
You can verify this by placing a protractor or the corner of a sheet of paper at the intersection to see if it fits perfectly.
For instance, consider the example of Corner Check:
The corner of your textbook is formed by two perpendicular lines meeting at a 90° angle.
Another case is the study of Crossroad Corners:
A grid-style street layout has roads intersecting perpendicularly, creating 90-degree corners.
How does the concept of Identifying Perpendicular Lines (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Corner Check
examples.content: The corner of your textbook is formed by two perpendicular lines meeting at a 90° angle.

examples.title: Crossroad Corners
examples.content: A grid-style street layout has roads intersecting perpendicularly, creating 90-degree corners.

slides.title: Lines in the Real World (Advanced)
slides.text:
Architects, engineers, and designers use parallel, perpendicular, and intersecting lines to create houses, bridges, and beautiful artworks.
Look around and find these lines!.
For instance, consider the example of Eiffel Tower:
The Eiffel Tower contains intersecting support beams and parallel vertical columns.
Another case is the study of Bridges:
Suspension bridges use parallel vertical cables and perpendicular steel trusses.
How does the concept of Lines in the Real World (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Eiffel Tower
examples.content: The Eiffel Tower contains intersecting support beams and parallel vertical columns.

examples.title: Bridges
examples.content: Suspension bridges use parallel vertical cables and perpendicular steel trusses.

slides.title: Drawing Geometric Lines (Advanced)
slides.text:
To draw parallel or perpendicular lines, we use a ruler (straightedge) and a set square (triangle ruler).
A set square helps us align a perfect 90-degree angle.
For instance, consider the example of Drawing Parallel Lines:
Slide a ruler along a fixed edge to draw two lines that maintain the same distance.
Another case is the study of Drawing Perpendiculars:
Place the 90° corner of a set square against a line, then draw along the vertical edge.
How does the concept of Drawing Geometric Lines (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Drawing Parallel Lines
examples.content: Slide a ruler along a fixed edge to draw two lines that maintain the same distance.

examples.title: Drawing Perpendiculars
examples.content: Place the 90° corner of a set square against a line, then draw along the vertical edge.

slides.title: Summary: Geometric Lines (Advanced)
slides.text:
Superb! You have mastered lines in geometry: Parallel lines (||, never meet), Intersecting lines (cross at a point), and Perpendicular lines (⊥, cross at exactly 90 degrees).
These concepts are the foundation of shape study and drafting.
For instance, consider the example of Key Symbols:
|| means Parallel. ⊥ means Perpendicular. Vertex: Intersection point.
Another case is the study of Daily Practice:
Scan your room today: count 5 parallel lines and 5 perpendicular lines in your furniture!
How does the concept of Summary: Geometric Lines (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Key Symbols
examples.content: || means Parallel. ⊥ means Perpendicular. Vertex: Intersection point.

examples.title: Daily Practice
examples.content: Scan your room today: count 5 parallel lines and 5 perpendicular lines in your furniture!

standard.q: What are parallel lines?
options: Lines that cross at exactly 90 degrees.
Lines that will never meet or cross.

standard.q: Which symbol represents that two lines are parallel?
What are perpendicular lines?
options: Lines that cross to form exactly 90-degree angles.
Lines that go in opposite directions.

standard.q: Which symbol represents that two lines are perpendicular?
What is the point where two intersecting lines cross called?
Which of the following is a real-world example of parallel lines?
Which of the following represents perpendicular lines?
options: The rails of a train track
The opposite sides of a book
The hands of a clock showing 12:20

standard.q: If two lines cross each other at a 45-degree angle, they are:
How many degrees are in the angles formed by perpendicular lines?
If line L1 || L2, and L1 is extended by 10 meters, will they cross?
options: Only if L2 is also extended.

standard.q: Which capital letter has parallel lines?
Which capital letter has perpendicular lines but no parallel lines?
Which capital letter contains intersecting lines that are NOT perpendicular?
Which tool is best for checking if two drawn lines are perpendicular?
How many intersection points do two parallel lines have?
How many intersection points do two intersecting lines have?
If line AB ⊥ CD, how many right angles are formed around the intersection?
Which of the following shapes has opposite sides that are parallel?
Which of the following shapes has adjacent sides that are perpendicular?
What type of lines are formed by the letter 'V'?
Which of the following is a property of a line?
options: It extends infinitely in both directions.

standard.q: Which of the following is a property of a line segment?
options: It has exactly two endpoints and a fixed length.
It has arrows on both ends.

standard.q: Which of the following statements is true?
options: Statement A: All perpendicular lines are intersecting, but not all intersecting lines are perpendicular.
Statement B: All intersecting lines are perpendicular, but not all perpendicular lines are intersecting.

standard.q: What geometric term describes lines that are in the same plane and never cross? (use lowercase)
Which letter has both parallel and perpendicular lines?

challenge.q: If line A || line B, and line B ⊥ line C, what is the relationship between line A and line C?
How many pairs of parallel lines are there in a regular hexagon?
In a right-angled triangle, how many pairs of perpendicular sides are there?
Which of the following statements is true?
options: Statement A: If two lines are parallel to the same line, they are parallel to each other.
Statement B: If two lines are perpendicular to the same line, they are perpendicular to each other.

challenge.q: How many points of intersection are formed by three mutually intersecting lines that do not pass through the same point?

performance.title: Drawing and Classifying Lines
performance.desc: Use a ruler to draw a line segment and label it AB. Then, draw a line CD parallel to AB (AB || CD). Next, draw a line EF perpendicular to AB (EF ⊥ AB) crossing both lines. Label all points of intersection. Color parallel lines blue and perpendicular lines red. Show it to your parent.
labels: Correct drawing of parallel lines labeled AB and CD
Correct drawing of perpendicular line EF forming 90° angles
Neatness, color coding, and correct labeling of points

pages:
Exercise 1: Drawing Parallel and Perpendicular Lines

Use your stylus to draw a parallel line and a perpendicular line for each line segment below.

Draw a line Parallel to AB:
A B
Draw a line Perpendicular to CD:
C D

Exercise 2: Classifying Lines in Capital Letters

Look at the capital letters below. State what types of lines they contain (Parallel, Perpendicular, or Intersecting but not Perpendicular) using your stylus.

1. The letter E contains:
2. The letter X contains:
3. The letter M contains:

Exercise 3: Line Riddles

Write the correct answer to the geometric riddles below using your stylus.

1. We are a pair of lines. We are in the same plane, we go in the same direction, and we never cross. What are we?
2. We cross each other and form four right angles. What special lines are we?

slides.title: Advanced Instrumentation (Advanced)
slides.text:
Scientific research relies on high-precision instruments that extend human senses and reduce measurement error.
This slide introduces key insights about Advanced Instrumentation.
For instance, consider the example of Calibration:
Comparing instrument readouts against a known standard to adjust accuracy.
Another case is the study of Precision:
The repeatability of measurements under unchanged conditions.
How does the concept of Advanced Instrumentation help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Advanced Vocabulary (Advanced)
slides.text:
Terms of precision measurement: Graduated cylinder, Triple beam balance, Calipers, and Meniscus.
This slide introduces key insights about Advanced Vocabulary.
For instance, consider the example of Graduated cylinder:
A narrow container with accurate marks used to measure fluid volumes.
Another case is the study of Meniscus:
The curve at the upper surface of a liquid in a tube.
How does the concept of Advanced Vocabulary help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Instrument Selection Strategy (Advanced)
slides.text:
Selecting tools requires matching the scale and physical properties of the subject with the resolution of the instrument.
This slide introduces key insights about Instrument Selection Strategy.
For instance, consider the example of Resolution:
The smallest increment of change that can be detected by a tool.
Another case is the study of Tolerance:
The allowable limit of variation in a physical measurement.
How does the concept of Instrument Selection Strategy help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Advanced Tool: Vernier Calipers (Advanced)
slides.text:
Vernier calipers measure internal, external, and depth dimensions with sub-millimeter precision.
This slide introduces key insights about Advanced Tool: Vernier Calipers.
For instance, consider the example of External Jaws:
Measuring the diameter of a clay sphere.
Another case is the study of Vernier Scale:
Reading the fractional division of a millimeter for high accuracy.
How does the concept of Advanced Tool: Vernier Calipers help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Advanced Tool: Microscope (Advanced)
slides.text:
Microscopes use compound lenses and light refraction to magnify objects up to 1000 times their size.
This slide introduces key insights about Advanced Tool: Microscope.
For instance, consider the example of Coarse Adjustment:
Focusing the specimen under low magnification power.
Another case is the study of Fine Adjustment:
Sharpening the focus detail under high magnification power.
How does the concept of Advanced Tool: Microscope help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Advanced Tool: Spring Scale (Advanced)
slides.text:
Spring scales measure force or weight in Newtons by stretching a calibrated internal spring.
This slide introduces key insights about Advanced Tool: Spring Scale.
For instance, consider the example of Force Measurement:
Measuring the pull force needed to drag a block on cardboard.
Another case is the study of Gravity Impact:
Shows the downward gravitational force acting on a mass.
How does the concept of Advanced Tool: Spring Scale help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Advanced Tool: Graduated Cylinder (Advanced)
slides.text:
Graduated cylinders provide much higher liquid volume accuracy than standard beakers or cups.
This slide introduces key insights about Advanced Tool: Graduated Cylinder.
For instance, consider the example of Material:
Often made of chemical resistant polypropylene or glass.
Another case is the study of Meniscus Reading:
Always read at the lowest point of the concave water surface.
How does the concept of Advanced Tool: Graduated Cylinder help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Advanced Tool: Digital Balance (Advanced)
slides.text:
Digital scales measure mass with high precision by converting force into digital electrical signals.
This slide introduces key insights about Advanced Tool: Digital Balance.
For instance, consider the example of Tare Function:
Resetting the scale to zero with a container on it to weigh only contents.
Another case is the study of Units:
Reads mass in grams, ounces, or grains.
How does the concept of Advanced Tool: Digital Balance help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

slides.title: Advanced Tool: Anemometer (Advanced)

Anemometers measure wind speed and direction, converting wind pressure into mechanical rotation.

slides.title: Advanced Tool: Hydrometer (Advanced)
slides.text:
Hydrometers measure the relative density (specific gravity) of liquids based on buoyancy concepts.

english.subtitle: Week 3: Collective Nouns & Final Consonant Blends [Advanced]

slides.title: What are Collective Nouns? (Advanced)
slides.text:
A collective noun is a word that names a group of people, animals, or things as a single unit or entity.
Even though the group has many members, we treat the noun as a single thing.
For instance, consider the example of For People:
A 'class' of students, a 'team' of players, an 'audience' of listeners.
Another case is the study of For Animals:
A 'flock' of birds, a 'herd' of cows, a 'school' of fish, a 'pack' of wolves.
How does the concept of What are Collective Nouns? (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: For People
examples.content: A 'class' of students, a 'team' of players, an 'audience' of listeners.

examples.title: For Animals
examples.content: A 'flock' of birds, a 'herd' of cows, a 'school' of fish, a 'pack' of wolves.

slides.title: Collective Nouns for Things (Advanced)
slides.text:
We also use specific collective nouns to group inanimate objects together.
Using the correct collective noun makes your English descriptive and precise.
For instance, consider the example of Everyday Objects:
A 'bunch' of keys, a 'set' of tools, a 'pack' of cards, a 'fleet' of ships.
Another case is the study of In a Sentence:
Matteo found a 'bunch' of bananas on the kitchen counter.
How does the concept of Collective Nouns for Things (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.content: A 'bunch' of keys, a 'set' of tools, a 'pack' of cards, a 'fleet' of ships.

examples.title: In a Sentence
examples.content: Matteo found a 'bunch' of bananas on the kitchen counter.

slides.title: Agreement Rules: Singular vs. Plural (Advanced)
slides.text:
Most of the time, collective nouns take singular verbs because we view the group acting together as one unit.
However, if the members are acting individually, we can use a plural verb.
For instance, consider the example of Singular Verb (Together):
The team 'is' playing well. (The group acts together -> Singular)
Another case is the study of Plural Verb (Individuals):
The family 'are' arguing about their dinner plans. (Members act separately -> Plural)
How does the concept of Agreement Rules: Singular vs. Plural (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Singular Verb (Together)
examples.content: The team 'is' playing well. (The group acts together -> Singular)

examples.title: Plural Verb (Individuals)
examples.content: The family 'are' arguing about their dinner plans. (Members act separately -> Plural)

slides.title: What are Final Consonant Blends? (Advanced)
slides.text:
A final consonant blend is when two consonant letters sit at the end of a word, and both consonant sounds are pronounced smoothly together.
Let's practice hearing and spelling these endings.
For instance, consider the example of The -st Blend:
/s/ and /t/ blend to make /st/ as in 'nest', 'fast', or 'vest'.
Another case is the study of The -nd Blend:
/n/ and /d/ blend to make /nd/ as in 'hand', 'sand', or 'pond'.
How does the concept of What are Final Consonant Blends? (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: The -st Blend
examples.content: /s/ and /t/ blend to make /st/ as in 'nest', 'fast', or 'vest'.

examples.title: The -nd Blend
examples.content: /n/ and /d/ blend to make /nd/ as in 'hand', 'sand', or 'pond'.

slides.title: Common Final Blends: -nd and -mp (Advanced)
slides.text:
Practice saying and spelling: -nd (wind, send, land, mind) and -mp (camp, jump, lamp, stamp).
Listen carefully to make sure you hear both ending sounds.
For instance, consider the example of -nd Sentences:
Matteo played on the wet 'sand' near the 'pond'.
Another case is the study of -mp Sentences:
Turn off the 'lamp' before you 'jump' into bed.
How does the concept of Common Final Blends: -nd and -mp (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: -nd Sentences
examples.content: Matteo played on the wet 'sand' near the 'pond'.

examples.title: -mp Sentences
examples.content: Turn off the 'lamp' before you 'jump' into bed.

slides.title: Common Final Blends: -st and -ft (Advanced)
slides.text:
Practice saying: -st (dust, last, post, best) and -ft (gift, lift, soft, draft).
S-blends and T-blends are very sharp at the end of words.
For instance, consider the example of -st Sentences:
She was the 'last' student to finish the reading 'test'.
Another case is the study of -ft Sentences:
He gave his mother a lovely 'gift' wrapped in 'soft' paper.
How does the concept of Common Final Blends: -st and -ft (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.content: She was the 'last' student to finish the reading 'test'.

examples.title: -ft Sentences
examples.content: He gave his mother a lovely 'gift' wrapped in 'soft' paper.

slides.title: Common Final Blends: -lk and -lt (Advanced)
slides.text:
Practice saying: -lk (milk, walk, talk) and -lt (belt, melt, salt).
These blends contain the letter 'l' and have a soft, flowing sound followed by a sharp ending.
For instance, consider the example of -lk Sentences:
Drink a glass of cold 'milk' every morning.
Another case is the study of -lt Sentences:
The ice will 'melt' quickly in the hot sun.
How does the concept of Common Final Blends: -lk and -lt (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.content: Drink a glass of cold 'milk' every morning.

examples.title: -lt Sentences
examples.content: The ice will 'melt' quickly in the hot sun.

slides.title: Analyzing Text: Cause & Effect (Advanced)
slides.text:
Informational texts often show Cause and Effect.
The Cause is *why* something happens (the reason).
The Effect is *what* happens (the result).
Look for clue words like: because, so, since, as a result.
For instance, consider the example of Identifying Cause:
Because the sun was hot, the ice cream melted. Cause: Sun was hot.
Another case is the study of Identifying Effect:
Because the sun was hot, the ice cream melted. Effect: Ice cream melted.

examples.content: Because the sun was hot, the ice cream melted. Cause: Sun was hot.

examples.title: Identifying Effect
examples.content: Because the sun was hot, the ice cream melted. Effect: Ice cream melted.

slides.title: Summary: Collectives & Final Blends (Advanced)
slides.text:
Wonderful! You have learned collective nouns for groups, verb agreement rules, final consonant blends (-nd, -mp, -st, -ft, -lk, -lt), and how to identify cause and effect in informational texts.
This slide introduces key insights about Summary: Collectives & Final Blends (Advanced).
For instance, consider the example of Key Grammar:
Collective: group as one (flock, class). Final blends: end of word. Cause: why. Effect: what.
Another case is the study of Daily English:
Practice: Find 3 collective nouns in your house today and use them in full sentences!
How does the concept of Summary: Collectives & Final Blends (Advanced) help us think critically?
Keep up your curiosity and check your facts as a scientist!

examples.title: Key Grammar
examples.content: Collective: group as one (flock, class). Final blends: end of word. Cause: why. Effect: what.

examples.title: Daily English
examples.content: Practice: Find 3 collective nouns in your house today and use them in full sentences!

standard.q: Which of the following is a collective noun for a group of fish?
Choose the correct collective noun: 'A ________ of birds flew over the lake.'
What is the collective noun for a group of players?
Which word represents a collective noun for keys?
Which collective noun is used for ships?
Choose the correct verb: 'The class ________ listening to the teacher.' (The class acts together)
What does a final consonant blend mean?
options: Two consonants at the beginning of a word.
Two consonants at the end of a word where both sounds are heard.
A vowel and a consonant sound.

standard.q: Which word ends with a final consonant blend?
Identify the final consonant blend in the word 'lamp'.
Which word ends with the -st blend?
Which word ends with the -ft blend?
Which word ends with the -lk blend?
In the sentence: 'The wind blew the sand.', which word has a final consonant blend?
In cause and effect, what does the 'Cause' represent?
In the sentence: 'Lina got wet because she forgot her umbrella.', what is the Cause?
In the sentence: 'Lina got wet because she forgot her umbrella.', what is the Effect?
Which word is a clue word for cause and effect?
What is the plural of 'wolf'?
Which collective noun is used for wolves?
Which word blends /l/ and /t/ at the end?
Which collective noun is best for a group of bees?
Complete the sentence: 'The ice will ________ in the hot sun.' (hint: ends with -lt)
Which of the following statements is true?
options: Statement A: Collective nouns name a group as a unit, and final blends occur at the end of words.
Statement B: Collective nouns occur at the end of words, and final blends name a group as a unit.

standard.q: What is the collective noun for a group of cows? (use lowercase)
Which final blend is in the word 'gift'?

challenge.q: Choose the correct sentence:
options: The pack of cards are lying on the table.
The pack of cards is lying on the table.
The pack card is lying on the table.
The card pack are lying on the table.

challenge.q: Which word contains both an initial consonant blend and a final consonant blend?
Identify cause and effect: 'The electricity went out, so the computer shut down.' What is the effect?
Which of the following statements is true?
options: Statement A: 'Audience' is a collective noun, and 'fast' has a final consonant blend.
Statement B: 'Audience' is a final consonant blend, and 'fast' is a collective noun.

challenge.q: Identify the final consonant blend in the word 'bench'.

performance.title: Collectives and Blends Chart
performance.desc: Create a list of 5 collective nouns and write a full sentence for each. In your sentences, highlight at least 5 words that end in a final consonant blend (e.g., 'The team [collective] practiced on the wet sand [final blend].'). Underline the final blends. Share it with your teacher.

Five final consonant blend words highlighted

Complete the spelling of the words using the final consonant blends: -nd, -st, -ft, or -mp.

1. sa____ (ground sand)
2. la____ (table light)
3. te____ (exam paper)
4. gi____ (present box)

filipino.subtitle: Week 3: Panghalip Panao at Elemento ng Kwento [Advanced]

slides.title: Ano ang Panghalip Panao? (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang panghalip panao ay mga salitang humahalili o pumapalit sa ngalan ng tao. Ginagamit ito upang hindi paulit-ulit ang pagbanggit sa pangalan. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Halimbawa 1: Si Ana ay masipag na bata. Siya ay palaging naglilinis ng bahay. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Halimbawa 2:

examples.title: Halimbawa 1
examples.content: Si Ana ay masipag na bata. Siya ay palaging naglilinis ng bahay.

examples.title: Halimbawa 2
examples.content: Sina Ben at ako ay maglalaro. Kami ay masayang nagtatakbo.

slides.title: Tatlong Panauhan ng Panghalip Panao (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang panghalip panao ay may tatlong panauhan batay sa taong nagsasalita, kinakausap, o pinag-uusapan: Una, Ikalawa, at Ikatlo. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Unang Panauhan (Speaker): Ako ay nag-aaral ng aking leksyon tuwing hapon. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Ikalawang Panauhan (Listener): Ikaw ba ang kumuha ng aklat ko sa lamesa? Paano nakakatulong ang konsepto ng Tatlong Panauhan ng Panghalip Panao (Advanced) sa ating kritikal na pag-iisip?

examples.title: Unang Panauhan (Speaker)
examples.content: Ako ay nag-aaral ng aking leksyon tuwing hapon.

examples.title: Ikalawang Panauhan (Listener)
examples.content: Ikaw ba ang kumuha ng aklat ko sa lamesa?

slides.title: Kailanan ng Panghalip Panao: Isahan (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang isahang kailanan ng panghalip panao ay tumutukoy sa iisang tao lamang. Ito ay ang 'ako', 'ikaw' (o 'ka'), at 'siya'. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Gamit ang 'siya': Siya ang nagbigay sa akin ng kendi kahapon sa klase. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Gamit ang 'ka': Kumain ka na ba ng masustansyang tanghalian ngayon?

examples.title: Gamit ang 'siya'
examples.content: Siya ang nagbigay sa akin ng kendi kahapon sa klase.

examples.title: Gamit ang 'ka'
examples.content: Kumain ka na ba ng masustansyang tanghalian ngayon?

slides.title: Kailanan ng Panghalip Panao: Maramihan (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang maramihang kailanan ay tumutukoy sa dalawa o higit pang tao. Ito ay ang 'kami', 'tayo', 'kayo', at 'sila'. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Kami vs Tayo: Kami ay mamamasyal (hindi kasama ang kausap). Tayo ay magdasal (kasama ang lahat). Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Gamit ang 'sila': Sila ay nagtatanim ng mga gulay sa likod-bahay ni Lolo Juan.

examples.title: Kami vs Tayo
examples.content: Kami ay mamamasyal (hindi kasama ang kausap). Tayo ay magdasal (kasama ang lahat).

examples.title: Gamit ang 'sila'
examples.content: Sila ay nagtatanim ng mga gulay sa likod-bahay ni Lolo Juan.

slides.title: Malalim na Pagsusuri ng Banghay ng Kwento (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang banghay ay may mga tiyak na bahagi: Simula, Tunggalian (suliranin), Kasukdulan (pinakamasidhing bahagi), Kakalasan (pababang aksyon), at Wakas. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Tunggalian (Conflict): Ang suliranin sa kwento. Halimbawa: Si Kuneho ay nahulog sa bitag. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Kasukdulan (Climax): Ang pinakapananabik na bahagi kung malulutas ba ang problema.

examples.title: Tunggalian (Conflict)
examples.content: Ang suliranin sa kwento. Halimbawa: Si Kuneho ay nahulog sa bitag.

examples.title: Kasukdulan (Climax)
examples.content: Ang pinakapananabik na bahagi kung malulutas ba ang problema.

slides.title: Pagsusuri ng Katotohanan at Opinyon (Advanced)
slides.text: Mahalagang malaman kung ang isang pahayag sa kwento ay katotohanan o opinyon upang maging kritikal na mambabasa. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Katotohanan: Ang araw ay sumisikat sa silangan araw-araw. (Katotohanan) Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Opinyon:

examples.title: Katotohanan
examples.content: Ang araw ay sumisikat sa silangan araw-araw. (Katotohanan)

examples.title: Opinyon
examples.content: Ang pagsikat ng araw ang pinakamasayang bahagi ng araw. (Opinyon)

slides.title: Buod: Panghalip at Kwento (Advanced)
slides.text: Binabati kita! Natutunan mo ang panghalip panao (panauhan at kailanan), mga bahagi ng banghay (tunggalian at kasukdulan), at ang pagkilala sa katotohanan at opinyon. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Tandaan: Panghalip panao: ako, siya, sila. Kasukdulan = climax. Katotohanan = may patunay. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Daily Challenge: Sumulat ng tatlong pangungusap tungkol sa iyong araw gamit ang iba't ibang panghalip panao! Paano nakakatulong ang konsepto ng Buod: Panghalip at Kwento (Advanced) sa ating kritikal na pag-iisip?

standard.q: Ano ang panghalip panao?
options: Salitang tumutukoy sa ngalan ng lugar.
Salitang ipinapalit sa ngalan ng tao.
Salitang naglalarawan ng kilos.
Salitang naglalarawan ng katangian.

standard.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang unang panauhan ng panghalip panao na isahan?
Ano ang kailanan ng panghalip panao na 'sila'?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang ikatlong panauhan na isahan?
Anong panghalip ang gagamitin kung kinakausap mo ang isang grupo ng mga bata?
Ano ang pagkakaiba ng 'kami' at 'tayo'?
options: Ang 'kami' ay isahan, ang 'tayo' ay maramihan.
Ang 'kami' ay hindi kasama ang kausap, ang 'tayo' ay kasama ang kausap.
Ang 'kami' ay para sa hayop, ang 'tayo' ay para sa tao.
Walang pagkakaiba.

standard.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang panghalip panao na isahan sa ikalawang panauhan?
Anong bahagi ng banghay ng kwento ang tumutukoy sa problema o suliranin ng tauhan?
Ano ang tawag sa pinakakapana-panabik o pinakamasidhing bahagi ng kwento?
Alin sa mga pahayag ang isang KATOTOHANAN?
options: Ayon sa pagsusuri, ang asukal ay natutunaw sa tubig.
Para sa akin, masarap ang bitter gourd.
Sa tingin ko ay uulan bukas.
Siguro ay masaya ang mamasyal sa parke.

standard.q: Alin sa mga pahayag ang isang OPINYON?
options: Ang watawat ng Pilipinas ay may kulay asul, pula, puti, at dilaw.
Para sa akin, ang Cariñosa ang pinakamahirap na sayaw.
Ang Lungsod ng Maynila ang kabisera ng Pilipinas.
Si Jose Rizal ay binaril sa Luneta.

standard.q: Ano ang kailanan ng panghalip panao sa pangungusap: 'Sila ay naglalaro ng basketbol.'?
Anong panauhan ang panghalip na 'ako'?
Anong panauhan ang panghalip na 'kayo'?
Alin sa mga sumusunod na panghalip ang HINDI kabilang sa maramihan?
Ano ang tinutukoy ng 'tunggalian' sa kwento?
options: Ang tagpuan ng kaganapan.
Ang labanan o problema ng bida laban sa ibang pwersa.
Ang katapusan ng kwento.
Ang mga diyalogo ng bida.

standard.q: Ano ang sumusunod na bahagi pagkatapos ng kasukdulan kung saan pababa ang aksyon ng kwento?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang nagpapakita ng opinyon gamit ang panandang salita?
options: Napatunayan na ang mundo ay bilog.
Sa aking palagay, maganda ang kanta.
Ayon sa balita, may bagyo.
Ayon sa doktor, uminom ng tubig.

standard.q: Anong panghalip panao ang angkop sa patlang: 'Si Matteo ay mabuting bata. ___ ay sumusunod sa magulang.'?
Anong panghalip panao ang angkop: 'Sina Ana, Ben, at ako ay naglinis ng silid. ___ ay napagod.'?
Alin ang pananda para sa katotohanan?
options: Ayon sa pagsusuri/dokumento

standard.q: Ilang panauhan mayroon ang panghalip panao?
Alin sa mga sumusunod na panghalip ang tama?
options: Pahayag A: Ang 'siya' ay isahan sa ikatlong panauhan, samantalang ang 'sila' ay maramihan sa ikatlong panauhan.
Pahayag B: Ang 'siya' ay maramihan sa ikatlong panauhan, samantalang ang 'sila' ay isahan sa ikatlong panauhan.

standard.q: Ano ang panghalip panao na ginagamit bilang pamalit sa ngalan ng nagsasalita? (use lowercase)
Anong panghalip ang angkop kung kakausapin mo ang iyong lolo at lola?

challenge.q: Suriin ang pangungusap: 'Sabi ni Ate Sarah sa amin, tayo ay dapat magtulungan upang sila ay matuwa.' Alin ang panghalip panao na maramihan na tumutukoy sa nagsasalita at kausap?
options: lahat ng nabanggit

challenge.q: Bakit mahalaga ang tunggalian sa isang kwento?
options: Upang ipakita ang magandang tagpuan.
Upang magkaroon ng hamon at interes sa daloy ng kwento na nagpapakita ng pag-unlad ng tauhan.
Upang wakasan agad ang kwento.
Upang malaman ang opinyon ng may-akda.

challenge.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang naglalaman ng katotohanan na napatunayan ng agham?
options: Para sa akin, masarap ang tubig kapag mainit.
Ang tubig ay binubuo ng dalawang atomo ng hydrogen at isang atomo ng oxygen (H2O).
Siguro ay may tubig sa ibang planeta.
Sa aking palagay, ang tubig ay kulay asul.

challenge.q: Alin sa mga pahayag ang tama?
options: Pahayag A: Ang kasukdulan ang pinakarurok ng kapanabikan sa kwento, kung saan haharapin ang tunggalian.
Pahayag B: Ang kasukdulan ang unang bahagi ng kwento kung saan ipinapakilala ang tauhan.

challenge.q: Ano ang kailanan ng panghalip panaong 'sila'?

performance.title: Pagsulat ng Kwento gamit ang Panghalip
performance.desc: Sumulat ng isang talata (may 5 pangungusap) tungkol sa isang masayang karanasan ng iyong pamilya. Gamitin ang mga panghalip panao na 'ako', 'kami', 'siya', at 'sila'. Bilugan ang mga ito at guhitan ang mga pandiwa. Tukuyin ang simula, kasukdulan, at wakas ng iyong isinulat. Ipakita ito sa iyong magulang.
labels: Wastong pagkakagamit ng apat na panghalip panao
Malinaw na pagkakaroon ng simula, kasukdulan, at wakas
Wastong baybay at bantas sa pagsulat

makabansa.subtitle: Week 3: Heograpiya at Rehiyon ng Pilipinas [Advanced]

slides.title: Ang Heograpiya ng Pilipinas (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang heograpiya ay ang pag-aaral ng pisikal na katangian, klima, at likas na yaman ng isang lugar. Ang Pilipinas ay isang kapuluan sa Timog-Silangang Asya. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Kapuluan (Archipelago): Ang kapuluan ay pangkat ng mga pulo na napapaligiran ng tubig. Ang bansa ay may higit 7,600 na pulo. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Lokasyon:

examples.title: Kapuluan (Archipelago)
examples.content: Ang kapuluan ay pangkat ng mga pulo na napapaligiran ng tubig. Ang bansa ay may higit 7,600 na pulo.

examples.title: Lokasyon
examples.content: Ang Pilipinas ay napapaligiran ng Karagatang Pasipiko at Dagat Timog Tsina.

slides.title: Ang Tatlong Pangkat ng Pulo: Luzon (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang Pilipinas ay nahahati sa tatlong malalaking pangkat ng pulo: Luzon, Visayas, at Mindanao. Ang Luzon ang pinakamalaki sa hilaga. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Mga Lalawigan sa Luzon: Kabilang sa Luzon ang mga rehiyon ng Ilocos, Central Luzon, at Bicol. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Kabisera (Capital):

examples.title: Mga Lalawigan sa Luzon
examples.content: Kabilang sa Luzon ang mga rehiyon ng Ilocos, Central Luzon, at Bicol.

examples.title: Kabisera (Capital)
examples.content: Matatagpuan sa Luzon ang Metro Manila na siyang sentro ng pamahalaan at kalakalan.

slides.title: Ang Tatlong Pangkat ng Pulo: Visayas (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang Visayas ang gitnang pangkat ng pulo. Binubuo ito ng mga katamtamang laki ng mga isla na magkakagrupo. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Pangunahing Isla: Kabilang dito ang mga isla ng Cebu, Bohol, Samar, at Leyte. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Kultura at Wika:

examples.title: Pangunahing Isla
examples.content: Kabilang dito ang mga isla ng Cebu, Bohol, Samar, at Leyte.

examples.title: Kultura at Wika
examples.content: Ang mga tao rito ay nagsasalita ng Bisaya, Hiligaynon, at Waray.

slides.title: Ang Tatlong Pangkat ng Pulo: Mindanao (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang Mindanao ang pinakatimog na pangkat ng pulo at pangalawang pinakamalaki. Kilala ito sa agrikultura at bundok. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Bundok Apo (Mount Apo): Matatagpuan sa Mindanao ang Bundok Apo na pinakamataas na bundok sa bansa. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Kulturang Muslim at Katutubo:

examples.title: Bundok Apo (Mount Apo)
examples.content: Matatagpuan sa Mindanao ang Bundok Apo na pinakamataas na bundok sa bansa.

examples.title: Kulturang Muslim at Katutubo
examples.content: Mayaman ang Mindanao sa tradisyong Islamiko at katutubong Lumad.

slides.title: Likas na Yaman at Klima ng Rehiyon (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang bawat rehiyon ay may sariling likas na yaman batay sa heograpiya nito. Mayroon tayong klimang tropikal (tag-init at tag-ulan). . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Likas na Yaman: Luzon: palay at ginto. Visayas: asukal at isda. Mindanao: saging at pinya. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Klimang Tropikal:

examples.title: Likas na Yaman
examples.content: Luzon: palay at ginto. Visayas: asukal at isda. Mindanao: saging at pinya.

examples.title: Klimang Tropikal
examples.content: Ang klimang tropikal ay nagbibigay-daan sa pagtatanim sa buong taon.

slides.title: Katutubong Musika at Sayaw sa Luzon (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang musika at sayaw sa Luzon ay nagpapakita ng impluwensyang Kastila at tradisyong katutubo sa kabundukan tulad ng sining sa Cordillera. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Carinosa sa Luzon: Tanyag ang Cariñosa sa mga kapatagan ng Luzon. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Sining sa Cordillera: Mga sayaw na may kasamang gangsa (gong) ilang pasasalamat sa masaganang ani. Paano nakakatulong ang konsepto ng Katutubong Musika at Sayaw sa Luzon (Advanced) sa ating kritikal na pag-iisip?

examples.title: Carinosa sa Luzon
examples.content: Tanyag ang Cariñosa sa mga kapatagan ng Luzon.

examples.title: Sining sa Cordillera
examples.content: Mga sayaw na may kasamang gangsa (gong) ilang pasasalamat sa masaganang ani.

slides.title: Musika at Sayaw sa Visayas at Mindanao (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang Visayas ay sikat sa gitara at kapistahan (Sinulog). Ang Mindanao ay kilala sa kulintang at sayaw na Singkil. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Singkil sa Mindanao: Isang maharlikang sayaw ng Mindanao na gumagamit ng mga kawayan at kulintang. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Dandansoy sa Visayas:

examples.title: Singkil sa Mindanao
examples.content: Isang maharlikang sayaw ng Mindanao na gumagamit ng mga kawayan at kulintang.

examples.title: Dandansoy sa Visayas
examples.content: Isang tanyag na awiting-bayan sa Visayas na nagpapakita ng pamamaalam.

slides.title: Makasaysayang Pook ng Bansa (Advanced)
slides.text: May mga makasaysayang pook sa bawat pangkat ng pulo: Barasoain Church (Luzon), Magellan's Cross (Visayas), at Fort Pilar (Mindanao). . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Magellan's Cross (Cebu): Ang krus na itinanim ni Ferdinand Magellan sa Cebu noong taong 1521. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Barasoain Church (Bulacan): Ang simbahan kung saan isinulat ang unang Saligang Batas ng Pilipinas. Paano nakakatulong ang konsepto ng Makasaysayang Pook ng Bansa (Advanced) sa ating kritikal na pag-iisip?

examples.title: Magellan's Cross (Cebu)
examples.content: Ang krus na itinanim ni Ferdinand Magellan sa Cebu noong taong 1521.

examples.title: Barasoain Church (Bulacan)
examples.content: Ang simbahan kung saan isinulat ang unang Saligang Batas ng Pilipinas.
