slides.title: Buod: Heograpiya at Kultura (Advanced)
slides.text: Kamangha-mangha! Natutunan mo ang heograpiya ng Pilipinas (Luzon, Visayas, Mindanao), katutubong musika at sayaw ng iba't ibang pangkat ng pulo, at ang mga makasaysayang pook nito. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Tandaan: Luzon (Hilaga), Visayas (Gitna), Mindanao (Timog). Magellan's Cross sa Cebu. Singkil sa Mindanao. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Hamon: Hanapin sa mapa ng Pilipinas ang Luzon, Visayas, at Mindanao at bilugan ang mga pangunahing lungsod ngayon! Paano nakakatulong ang konsepto ng Buod: Heograpiya at Kultura (Advanced) sa ating kritikal na pag-iisip?

examples.title: Tandaan
examples.content: Luzon (Hilaga), Visayas (Gitna), Mindanao (Timog). Magellan's Cross sa Cebu. Singkil sa Mindanao.

examples.title: Hamon
examples.content: Hanapin sa mapa ng Pilipinas ang Luzon, Visayas, at Mindanao at bilugan ang mga pangunahing lungsod ngayon!

standard.q: Ano ang tawag sa pangkat ng mga pulo na napapaligiran ng tubig, tulad ng Pilipinas?
options: Kapuluan o Arkipelago

standard.q: Ilan ang pangunahing pangkat ng pulo sa Pilipinas?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pinakatimog na pangkat ng pulo sa Pilipinas?
Alin ang pinakamalaking pangkat ng pulo na matatagpuan sa hilagang bahagi ng bansa?
Saang pangkat ng pulo matatagpuan ang pambansang kabisera na Metro Manila?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang pangunahing isla sa pangkat ng Visayas?
Matatagpuan sa Mindanao ang pinakamataas na bundok sa Pilipinas. Ano ito?
Anong uri ng klima mayroon ang Pilipinas dahil sa heograpikal na lokasyon nito?
options: Klimang tropikal (tag-init at tag-ulan)

standard.q: Anong makasaysayang pook na may malaking krus ang matatagpuan sa Cebu sa Visayas?
Saang pangkat ng pulo nagmula ang maharlikang sayaw na Singkil?
Alin sa mga sumusunod na awitin ang tanyag na awiting-bayan mula sa Visayas?
options: Leron Leron Sinta
Atin Cu Pung Singsing

standard.q: Anong anyong tubig ang matatagpuan sa silangang bahagi ng Pilipinas?
options: Dagat Timog Tsina

standard.q: Aling simbahan sa Bulacan (Luzon) ang naging saksi sa pagsulat ng unang Saligang Batas ng bansa?
options: Simbahan ng Quiapo

standard.q: Anong instrumentong tanso ang tanyag sa musika ng Mindanao?
options: Kulintang o Gong

standard.q: Ano ang pangunahing wika na sinasalita ng mga tao sa rehiyon ng Cebu at Davao?
options: Cebuano o Bisaya

standard.q: Aling likas na yaman ang tanyag sa malalawak na bukirin sa Gitnang Luzon?
options: Ginto at Pilak
Pinya at Saging

standard.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod na anyong lupa ang kilala sa Bohol sa Visayas?
Saang pangkat ng pulo matatagpuan ang Zamboanga at Fort Pilar?
Ilang isla mayroon ang Pilipinas sa pinakahulin pag-aaral ng pamahalaan?
Ano ang pangunahing industriya sa Mindanao dahil sa matabang lupa nito?
Alin sa mga sumusunod ang anyong tubig sa kanlurang bahagi ng bansa?
options: Dagat Timog Tsina o Kanlurang Dagat ng Pilipinas

standard.q: Anong sayaw ang tanyag sa Visayas na ginagawa sa pagdiriwang ng Sinulog?
Alin sa mga sumusunod na pahayag ang tama?
options: Pahayag A: Ang Luzon, Visayas, at Mindanao ay kinakatawan ng tatlong bituin sa watawat ng Pilipinas.
Pahayag B: Ang Luzon, Visayas, at Mindanao ay kinakatawan ng walong sinag ng araw sa watawat ng Pilipinas.

standard.q: Ano ang pinakamataas na bundok sa Pilipinas na matatagpuan sa Mindanao? (simulan sa malaking titik)
Sino ang explorer na nagdala ng Magellan's Cross sa Cebu?

challenge.q: Bakit nahahati sa tatlong malalaking pangkat ang bansa at paano ito nakaapekto sa kultura natin?
options: Dahil tayo ay arkipelago na pinaghihiwalay ng mga anyong tubig, kaya nagkaroon ng iba't ibang wika, kultura, at tradisyon ngunit nagkakaisa pa rin bilang isang bansa.
Dahil magkakaaway ang mga rehiyon.
Dahil walang kalsada noon sa buong Pilipinas.
Dahil hindi tayo magkakapareho ng lahi.

challenge.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang nagpapakita ng tamang heograpikal na pagkakasunud-sunod mula Hilaga patungong Timog?
Ano ang kahalagahan ng Karagatang Pasipiko sa heograpiya at klima ng Pilipinas?
options: Wala itong kinalaman sa bansa.
Nagdadala ito ng ulan at mga bagyo, at nagsisilbing pangunahing daanan ng kalakalan sa silangan.
Ginagawa nitong malamig ang bansa sa buong taon.
Nilalamig ang mga isda doon.

challenge.q: Alin sa mga sumusunod na pahayag ang totoo?
options: Pahayag A: Ang Chocolate Hills ay nasa Visayas, at ang Banaue Rice Terraces ay nasa Luzon.
Pahayag B: Ang Chocolate Hills ay nasa Luzon, at ang Banaue Rice Terraces ay nasa Visayas.

challenge.q: Saang pangkat ng pulo matatagpuan ang bulkang Mayon na may perpektong kono? (use capital letter)

performance.title: Pagguhit ng Mapa ng Pilipinas
performance.desc: Kumuha ng isang malinis na papel. Iguhit ang simpleng balangkas ng mapa ng Pilipinas. Kulayan ng dilaw ang Luzon, asul ang Visayas, at berde ang Mindanao. Markahan kung saan matatagpuan ang Mayon Volcano (Luzon), Magellan's Cross (Visayas), at Mount Apo (Mindanao) gamit ang mga simbolo. Ipakita ito sa iyong magulang.
labels: Tatlong pangkat ng pulo na may tamang kulay (dilaw, asul, berde)
Monumento o makasaysayang pook na minarkahan sa mapa gamit ang simbolo
Malinis na pagkakaguhit at wastong labeling ng mga rehiyon

gmrc.title: GMRC
gmrc.subtitle: Week 3: Paggalang sa Pamilya at Pagmamahal sa Bayan [Advanced]

slides.title: Ang Pamilya bilang Haligi ng Lipunan (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang pamilya ang pinakapayak at pinakamahalagang yunit ng lipunan. Ang paggalang sa loob ng pamilya ay nagtatayo ng matatag na bansa. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Bayanihan sa Tahanan: Ang pagtulong ng bawat isa sa mga gawaing-bahay ay nagdadala ng kaayusan. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Pakikipagkapwa:

examples.title: Bayanihan sa Tahanan
examples.content: Ang pagtulong ng bawat isa sa mga gawaing-bahay ay nagdadala ng kaayusan.

examples.title: Pakikipagkapwa
examples.content: Ang batang magalang sa bahay ay magiging magalang sa kanyang paaralan.

slides.title: Paggalang sa mga Nakatatanda sa Pamilya (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang mga nakatatanda ay mayaman sa karanasan. Ipakita natin ang paggalang sa pamamagitan ng magalang na pananalita at pakikinig. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Pagsunod sa Bilin: Pakikinig sa mga paalala ni Lolo tungkol sa kaligtasan sa daan. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Pagsasabi ng 'Mano po':

examples.title: Pagsunod sa Bilin
examples.content: Pakikinig sa mga paalala ni Lolo tungkol sa kaligtasan sa daan.

examples.title: Pagsasabi ng 'Mano po'
examples.content: Pagmamano kay Lola Rosa upang humingi ng kanyang bendisyon at paggabay.

slides.title: Pagkamakabayan (Patriotism) (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang pagkamakabayan ay ang pagmamahal, paggalang, at katapatan sa ating bansa. Naipapakita ito sa paggalang sa watawat at pambansang awit. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Lupang Hinirang: Pagtayo nang tuwid habang inaawit ang Lupang Hinirang nang may puso. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Watawat ng Pilipinas:

examples.title: Lupang Hinirang
examples.content: Pagtayo nang tuwid habang inaawit ang Lupang Hinirang nang may puso.

examples.title: Watawat ng Pilipinas
examples.content: Pag-iingat sa watawat na hindi ito sumayad sa sahig o mabasa ng ulan.

slides.title: Paggalang sa mga Pambansang Sagisag (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang mga pambansang sagisag ay kumakatawan sa ating pagkakakilanlan tulad ng Sampaguita, Narra, at Philippine Eagle. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Pambansang Puno: Narra: Ang punong Narra ay sumisimbolo sa katatagan at lakas ng mga Pilipino. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Pambansang Bulaklak: Ang Sampaguita ay sumisimbolo sa kalinisan ng puso at kababaang-loob.

examples.title: Pambansang Puno: Narra
examples.content: Ang punong Narra ay sumisimbolo sa katatagan at lakas ng mga Pilipino.

examples.title: Pambansang Bulaklak
examples.content: Ang Sampaguita ay sumisimbolo sa kalinisan ng puso at kababaang-loob.

slides.title: Pangangalaga sa Pampublikong Ari-arian (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang mga pampublikong ari-arian tulad ng parke at aklatan ay pagmamay-ari ng lahat. Ang pag-aalaga rito ay tanda ng mabuting asal. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Huwag Magsusulat sa Desk: Pagpapanatiling malinis sa mga upuan sa paaralan nang walang sulat. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Pagtatapon sa Basurahan:

examples.title: Huwag Magsusulat sa Desk
examples.content: Pagpapanatiling malinis sa mga upuan sa paaralan nang walang sulat.

examples.title: Pagtatapon sa Basurahan
examples.content: Pagtatapon ng kalat sa tamang basurahan sa mga pampublikong plasa.

slides.title: Mabuting Mamamayan (Good Citizenship) (Advanced)
slides.text: Ang mabuting mamamayan ay sumusunod sa batas-trapiko, nagreresiklo, at tumutulong sa mga nangangailangan sa barangay. . Halimbawa, tingnan natin ang tungkol sa Bayanihan: Pagtulong sa paglilinis ng kanal upang maiwasan ang baha sa komunidad. Ang isa pang kaso ay ang pag-aaral ng Pagsunod sa Alituntunin: Pagtawid sa tamang tawiran (pedestrian lane) upang maiwasan ang sakuna.

examples.title: Bayanihan
examples.content: Pagtulong sa paglilinis ng kanal upang maiwasan ang baha sa komunidad.

examples.title: Pagsunod sa Alituntunin
examples.content: Pagtawid sa tamang tawiran (pedestrian lane) upang maiwasan ang sakuna.

slides.title: Story: The Honest Woodcutter - Part 1
slides.text: Once upon a time, a poor woodcutter lived in a small cottage near a deep forest. He worked hard every day, cutting down old trees for firewood to feed his family. One afternoon, as he was chopping a tree branch near the bank of a deep, fast-flowing river, his iron axe slipped from his sweaty hands and splashed into the water. The woodcutter sat by the muddy bank and began to weep. He was very sad because the river was deep, and he could not swim. The iron axe was his only tool, and he could not afford to buy a new one. Without his axe, he could not earn any money to buy food. Suddenly, a beautiful water spirit rose up from the bubbles. The woodcutter was very poor but worked hard every day to support his family. He walked to the forest early in the morning and spent hours cutting wood. When his axe fell into the deep river, he felt helpless because he did not have money to buy a new one, and without it, he could not earn a living.

slides.title: Story: The Honest Woodcutter - Part 2
slides.text: The water spirit saw his tears and asked, 'Why are you crying, my friend?' The woodcutter explained his trouble. Hearing this, the spirit dove down into the deep water and returned holding a shining golden axe. 'Is this yours?' she asked. 'No,' replied the honest woodcutter. 'That is not my axe.' The spirit dove again and returned with a beautiful silver axe. 'Is this yours?' she asked. 'No,' he said. Finally, she dove a third time and brought up his old, rusty iron axe. 'Yes! That is mine!' he cried happily. The spirit smiled. She was so pleased with his honesty that she decided to reward him. 'Take all three axes,' she said, placing them in his hands. The woodcutter was surprised to see a beautiful water fairy rise from the river, holding a shining golden axe. The fairy asked if the gold axe was his, but the honest woodcutter immediately said no. He did not let the sight of wealth tempt him, choosing to tell the truth instead of taking something that was not his.

slides.title: Story: The Honest Woodcutter - Part 3
slides.text: The woodcutter thanked the spirit and returned to his village with the gold, silver, and iron axes. He was now rich, but he remained a humble and kind man. A greedy neighbor heard his story and wanted to get rich too. He went to the river bank, threw his iron axe into the water, and began crying. The water spirit rose up and dove down, bringing a golden axe. 'Is this yours?' she asked. 'Yes, yes! That is mine!' lied the greedy neighbor, reaching for it. The spirit was angry at his lie. She took the golden axe and disappeared under the water, leaving the greedy neighbor with nothing, not even his own iron axe. He walked home empty-handed and sad. The fairy was so pleased with the woodcutter's honesty that she gave him all three axes as a reward. The woodcutter returned home happily and shared the good news with his family. He proved that honesty is always the best policy, and that being truthful brings respect, peace of mind, and unexpected rewards in the end.

slides.title: Vocabulary & Word Study
slides.text:
Vocabulary:
• Woodcutter - A person who cuts down trees for firewood.
• Wept - Cried past tense.
• Afford - Have enough money to pay for something.

Observe how the prefix 'wood-' and suffix '-cutter' combine to form a new noun!

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. Why did the woodcutter cry?
2. What three axes did the water spirit bring up?
3. Why did the water spirit reward him with all three axes?

slides.title: Moral Lesson & Reflection
slides.text:
Moral Focus: Honesty is the best policy.

The spirit was so pleased with the woodcutter's honesty that she gave him all three axes as a reward. Honesty brings respect and true rewards, whereas greed can lose us even what we have.

Discuss: When is a time you chose to tell the truth?

slides.title: Poem: The Whispering Wind - Part 1
slides.text:
I hear the wind go whispering by,
Under the blue and open sky.
It tells a tale of distant trees,
And dancing leaves and busy bees.

When I walk outside in the afternoon, I can feel a gentle breeze brushing against my cheeks. The wind makes a soft whistling sound as it moves through the green leaves of the big mango trees. It seems to carry stories from far-away places that I have never visited. The leaves dance and shake, and the colorful flowers nod their heads as if they are listening to the wind's secrets. It is a peaceful, beautiful sight that makes me feel happy and relaxed. I stand still and listen closely to the whispering sounds of nature around me, enjoying the calm and quiet day. The wind's whispers are a gentle reminder of the beauty and life that surrounds us in nature. It travels over mountains and seas, carrying seeds and helping plants reproduce. It also helps cool the hot earth, bringing comfort to animals and people. We can learn a lot by listening quietly to the sounds of nature.

slides.title: Poem: The Whispering Wind - Part 2
slides.text:
It travels far, it travels wide,
With secrets locked up deep inside.
It has no hands, it has no feet,
Yet sings a song so soft and sweet.

The wind is a mysterious traveler. It can cross high mountains, fly over deep oceans, and run through busy city streets without ever stopping. It has no physical body, so we cannot see it with our eyes, but we can see the results of its work. It pushes sailboats across the water and lifts colorful kites high into the air. When it is gentle, it sings a sweet song, rustling the grass. But when it is strong, it can roar like a lion. The wind is a powerful force of nature that connects all parts of the world. It is always moving, bringing changes to the weather. The wind also plays with the colorful kites that children fly in the park, lifting them high into the blue sky. It fills the sails of boats, helping them travel across the lakes and oceans. The wind is a powerful but invisible force of nature that helps us in many different ways every day.

slides.title: Poem: The Whispering Wind - Part 3
slides.text:
It cools the earth on hot summer days,
And pushes clouds in gentle ways.
So listen close when breezes blow,
And learn the secrets winds may know.

During hot afternoons, the cool breeze is a wonderful gift. It helps lower the temperature and makes us feel refreshed. The wind also plays a major role in our weather, pushing clouds across the sky to bring rain to thirsty crops. Scientists study the wind's speed and direction to predict the weather. By observing how the wind moves, we learn about physics and earth science. So next time you feel a breeze, close your eyes and listen to the whispering wind, appreciating the natural wonders of our environment. It has many lessons to teach us if we pay attention. The wind's cycle is a natural part of our weather system, bringing rain clouds to water the fields and clean the air. It works together with the sun and water to keep our planet green and healthy. By observing the wind, we can understand the science of weather and appreciate the delicate balance of our environment.

slides.title: Rhyme Scheme & Phonics
slides.text:
Phonics Focus: Rhyming Words
Let's identify the rhyming pairs in the poem:
• by / sky
• trees / bees
• wide / inside
• feet / sweet

Rhymes help create a natural rhythm in poems.

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. What color is the sky in the poem?
2. Name two things the wind tells tales about.
3. What secrets does the wind carry?

slides.title: Moral Focus: Observation
slides.text:
Nature is full of quiet beauty. By slowing down and listening to the wind or observing the leaves, we learn to appreciate the world around us.

Discuss: Close your eyes for one minute. What sounds of nature can you hear around you right now?

slides.title: Tongue Twister Fun - Part 1
slides.text: Welcome to our final speech gym challenge! Today we will visit a beautiful sandy seashore where a girl named Sally sells shells. Sally sells seashells by the seashore. The shells she sells are surely seashells. So if she sells shells on the seashore, I'm sure she sells seashore shells. Let's try saying this together: 'She sells seashells by the seashore.' Make sure you make a clean distinction between the soft /s/ sound in 'sells' and the soft /sh/ sound in 'shells'. Say it three times, starting slowly and then speeding up! It is a great exercise to train your tongue and lips for clear pronunciation and enunciation. Speak clearly to show your confidence. To make a clean sound, practice repeating these words slowly with your classmates. Sally worked hard to collect the most beautiful shells, placing them carefully in her basket. She wanted to show them to her friends and teach them about the different types of sea creatures that live in the ocean, sharing her knowledge.

slides.title: Tongue Twister Fun - Part 2
slides.text: Next, let's meet our old friend Peter Piper again. He is picking peppers in the garden. Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers. Did Peter Piper pick a peck of pickled peppers? If Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers, where's the peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked? A peck is a unit of dry measure, about two gallons! That is a lot of peppers. Let's practice saying the 'P' sounds: 'Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.' Make sure your lips pop clearly for each 'P' sound. Try saying it three times rapidly without stumbling or mixing up the words. Polly the puppy watched Peter and barked happily at him. Practicing these tongue twisters is a fun way to improve our pronunciation and speaking skills. It helps us speak clearly when we read books, ask questions in class, or tell stories to our family. Remember to focus on the different sounds, letting your tongue and lips move smoothly and accurately as you practice.

slides.title: Tongue Twister Fun - Part 3
slides.text: Finally, let's try a new twister about silly sheep sleeping in a shed. Six silly sheep slept soundly in the wooden shed. The sheep should sleep in the shed, not in the grass. Let's say: 'Six silly sheep slept soundly in the shed.' Say it as fast as you can! If you make a mistake, don't worry. Just take a deep breath, slow down, and try again. Practicing these tongue twisters daily helps improve your reading speed, speech clarity, and verbal confidence. Announcers, actors, and teachers practice them to speak clearly. You are doing a wonderful job. Keep up the good work and have fun with speech! Practice makes perfect. Speech exercises also help us build confidence when speaking in front of others. By practicing regularly, we can overcome our speaking difficulties and express our ideas clearly and effectively. Don't be afraid to make mistakes; just slow down, breathe, and try again, showing determination and patience as you learn. Clear speaking is a superpower that helps us communicate better.

slides.title: Speech & Pronunciation Practice
slides.text:
Phonics Focus: S-blend / Sh-sound contrast

Tongue twisters help train our vocal cords and tongue muscles to enunciate clearly. Practice saying 'sells' (soft /s/) and 'shells' (soft /sh/) back-to-back.

slides.title: Comprehension & Speech Tasks
slides.text:
Practice Activities:
1. Say Twister 1 three times, slowly increasing your speed.
2. Say Twister 2 without stumbling on the 'P' sound.
3. What does 'peck' mean? (A unit of dry measure).

slides.title: Focus: Perseverance
slides.text:
Learning to speak clearly takes practice. Don't worry if you slip up; tongue twisters are meant to be challenging! Keep trying and have fun with it.

Discuss: Why is it important to speak slowly and clearly when giving a speech?

thursday.title: Dr. Jose Rizal: The Avid Reader

slides.title: Biography: Dr. Jose Rizal - Part 1
slides.text: Dr. Jose Rizal is the national hero of the Philippines, and he was an exceptionally smart and curious boy. When he was young, his family called him Pepe. Pepe lived in Calamba, Laguna, and he loved to read books more than playing outside. Back then, books were rare and expensive. Pepe's mother, Teodora Alonso, was his very first teacher. She taught him how to read the alphabet and read him stories every night under the warm light of an oil lamp. Pepe listened carefully to every word, fascinated by the stories of distant places and heroes. He realized that reading was the key to understanding the world. He read every single book he could find. Jose's mother was his first teacher, and she taught him how to read the alphabet and pray. She noticed his love for books and encouraged him to read stories that taught moral lessons. Jose spent hours reading, letting his imagination travel to distant lands and learning about different cultures and ideas from around the world.

slides.title: Biography: Dr. Jose Rizal - Part 2
slides.text: One quiet evening, Pepe's mother read him a famous fable about a young moth. The young moth was fascinated by the bright, glowing flame of a candle. The mother moth warned her child: 'Do not fly too close to the flame, my child, or your wings will burn!' But the young moth did not listen. It was attracted to the light, flew too close, and its wings burned, causing it to fall into the hot oil. Pepe sat in silence, watching the real moths fly around their oil lamp. He never forgot this story. He learned that we must listen to the wise advice of our parents and teachers to keep ourselves safe from danger. He was a good boy. Jose was a very bright student who loved to learn languages, science, and history. He believed that education was the key to helping his country and improving the lives of his people. He studied hard in different schools and universities, always seeking to gain new knowledge and skills that he could use to help others.

slides.title: Biography: Dr. Jose Rizal - Part 3
slides.text: As Pepe grew up, his love for reading and learning never stopped. He went to school in Manila and later traveled to Europe to study medicine, becoming a skilled eye doctor. He also learned to speak over twenty languages! He wrote two famous novels, 'Noli Me Tangere' and 'El Filibusterismo', which helped show the difficulties of the Filipino people and inspired them to seek independence. He became a great artist, writer, and hero. Jose Rizal proved that reading books can open up the entire world to us and give us the power to help our country. He is an inspiration for all Filipino children to study hard. We honor him today. Jose Rizal's books and writings inspired the Filipino people to unite and fight for their freedom and rights. He remains a national hero because of his patriotism, intelligence, and dedication to education. His story teaches us that reading and learning are powerful tools that can change our lives and help our country succeed.

slides.title: Vocabulary & Word Study
slides.text:
Vocabulary:
• National Hero - A person recognized for their exceptional contribution to their country.
• Attracted - Drawn or pulled towards something.
• Advice - Recommendations or guidance offered.

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. Who was Pepe's first teacher?
2. What animal was the main character in the story Pepe's mother read?
3. What did Pepe learn from the story of the moth?

slides.title: Focus: Lifelong Learning
slides.text:
Dr. Jose Rizal became a doctor, a writer, an artist, and a hero because he never stopped reading and learning. Books open up the entire world to us.

Discuss: Like Pepe, what is a story your parents or teachers told you that you still remember?

slides.title: Story: The Boy Who Cried Wolf - Part 1
slides.text: Once, a young shepherd boy was given the duty of tending a flock of sheep on a grassy hill near a quiet forest. The job was lonely, and the boy got very bored sitting on the hill all day with nothing to do. To amuse himself, he decided to play a trick on the villagers. He ran down towards the village, waving his arms and shouting at the top of his lungs, 'Wolf! Wolf! A wolf is chasing the sheep!' The villagers stopped their work, grabbed sticks and axes, and rushed up the hill to help him. When they arrived, they found the sheep grazing peacefully, and the boy laughed at them. The villagers were very angry at him. The boy felt very lonely and bored on the hillside, watching the sheep eat grass all day. He wanted some excitement and attention, so he decided to play a trick on the villagers. He did not think about the consequences of his actions, choosing to tell a lie just to see the people run to help.

slides.title: Story: The Boy Who Cried Wolf - Part 2
slides.text: The villagers warned the boy, 'Do not cry wolf when there is no wolf!' They went back to their work. However, a few days later, the shepherd boy got bored again. He decided to play the same trick. He ran down the hill screaming, 'Wolf! Wolf!' The villagers rushed up the hill again. Once more, they found no wolf, only the boy laughing at their worried faces. 'A liar will not be believed, even when he tells the truth!' warned the wise village elder. The boy just ignored the warning and kept laughing. Then, one afternoon, a real, hungry wolf came out from the dark forest and attacked the sheep. The boy was very scared. The boy laughed at the angry villagers, thinking it was a very funny joke. He did not realize that by telling lies, he was losing their trust and respect. The villagers warned him that lying is wrong and that nobody believes a liar, even when he tells the truth. But the boy did not listen to their advice.

slides.title: Story: The Boy Who Cried Wolf - Part 3
slides.text: The boy was terrified. He ran towards the village, screaming louder than ever, 'Help! Wolf! Wolf! A real wolf is eating the sheep! Please help!' The villagers heard his cries, but they smiled and said to each other, 'He is just playing another trick to laugh at us. We will not go.' Nobody went up the hill to help him. The wolf killed many sheep and scattered the flock into the forest. The boy sat on the hill and cried, realizing his lies had cost him his flock. He learned that when we lie, we destroy the trust of others. Once trust is broken, people will not believe us even when we tell the truth. Honesty is very important. The boy shivered in fear as the wolf came closer, but his cries were ignored by the villagers. He realized too late that his lies had cost him the safety of his sheep and the trust of his community. He learned that honesty is essential, and that we must always tell the truth to keep our friends' trust.

slides.title: Vocabulary & Word Study
slides.text:
Vocabulary:
• Shepherd - A person who guards and tends sheep.
• Tending - Caring for or watching over.
• Amuse - Entertain or cause amusement.

Observe how 'shepherd' combines 'sheep' and 'herd'!

slides.title: Comprehension Questions
slides.text:
Questions:
1. Why did the boy cry 'Wolf' when there was no wolf?
2. Why didn't the villagers come the third time?
3. What happened to the sheep at the end?

slides.title: Moral Lesson & Reflection
slides.text:
Moral Focus: Trustworthiness.

A liar will not be believed, even when they speak the truth. Trust takes a long time to build but can be destroyed in a single moment.

Discuss: Why is it important for people to be able to trust your word?

slides.title: Kuwento: Ang Langgam at ang Tipaklong - Bahagi 1
slides.text: Noong panahon ng tag-araw, ang buong kagubatan ay napakaganda at puno ng pagkain. Habang si Tipaklong ay masayang kumakanta at sumasayaw sa ilalim ng init ng araw, si Langgam naman ay abalang-abala sa paghahanap at paghahakot ng mga butil ng palay at prutas patungo sa kanyang munting bahay. 'Langgam, halika muna rito at magsaya tayo! Napakaraming pagkain ngayon, bakit ka nagpapapagod?' tanong ni Tipaklong habang tumutugtog ng gitara. Sumagot si Langgam, 'Nag-iipon ako ng pagkain para sa darating na tag-ulan upang hindi kami magutom. Dapat mag-ipon ka na rin, Tipaklong.' Ngunit tinawanan lamang siya ni Tipaklong at nagpatuloy sa paglalaro.

slides.title: Kuwento: Ang Langgam at ang Tipaklong - Bahagi 2
slides.text: Mabilis na lumipas ang mga araw at dumating ang panahon ng tag-ulan. Ulan dito, ulan doon, at naging napakalamig ng buong kagubatan. Nabasa ang mga dahon at walang makitang pagkain sa paligid dahil nalubog sa tubig ang mga damo. Ligtas na nanatili si Langgam sa loob ng kanyang tuyo at mainit na tahanan, kasama ang kanyang pamilya habang masayang kumakain ng mga butil na naipon nila noong tag-araw. Samantala, si Tipaklong ay gutom na gutom at nanginginig sa lamig sa labas, walang makitang masisilungan at walang makain.

slides.title: Kuwento: Ang Langgam at ang Tipaklong - Bahagi 3
slides.text: Dahil sa matinding gutom, lumapit si Tipaklong sa bahay ni Langgam at kumatok. 'Kaibigang Langgam, pakiusap at bigyan mo ako kahit kaunting pagkain. Nanginginig na ako sa gutom,' pakiusap niya. Nakaramdam ng awa si Langgam, kaya pinapasok niya si Tipaklong at binigyan ng mainit na pagkain at tuyong dahon. 'Salamat, Langgam. Ngayon ay naintindihan ko na kung bakit ka nagtrabaho noong tag-araw habang ako ay naglaro lamang,' wika ni Tipaklong nang may pagsisisi. Nangako si Tipaklong na sa susunod na tag-araw, mag-iipon din siya ng pagkain tulad ng kanyang masipag na kaibigan.

slides.title: Talasalitaan at Pag-aaral ng Salita
slides.text: Mga Bokabularyo: • Tag-araw - Panahon ng init at sikat ng araw. • Paghahakot - Pagsasama-sama o pagdadala ng mga bagay sa isang lugar. • Pagsisisi - Pakiramdam ng lungkot dahil sa maling desisyong ginawa. Tutok sa Phonics: Magsanay sa pagbigkas ng tunog ng 'l' sa 'langgam', 'lamig', at 'laro'.

slides.title: Mga Tanong sa Pag-unawa
slides.text: Mga Tanong: 1. Ano ang ginagawa ni Langgam habang naglalaro si Tipaklong? 2. Ano ang nangyari kay Tipaklong nang dumating ang tag-ulan? 3. Paano tinulungan ni Langgam si Tipaklong sa dulo ng kwento?

slides.title: Mabuting Aral at Pagninilay
slides.text: Kaisipan: Ang pagiging masipag at handa sa hinaharap ay nagliligtas sa atin sa hirap. May oras para sa paglalaro at may oras para sa pagtatrabaho. Ang pagiging handa ay nagpapakita ng katalinuhan, habang ang pagtulong sa nangangailangan ay nagpapakita ng kabaitan. Talakayin: Ano ang mga aralin sa paaralan na maaari mong pag-aralan nang maaga upang maging handa sa pagsusulit?

slides.title: Kuwento: Si Juan Tamad - Bahagi 1
slides.text: Sa isang maliit na baryo, may isang batang lalaki na nagngangalang Juan. Dahil sa kanyang labis na katamaran, binansagan siya ng kanyang mga kapitbahay na si Juan Tamad. Tuwing inuutusan siya ng kanyang ina, lagi siyang nagdadahilan o gumagawa ng paraan upang hindi matapos ang gawain. Isang mainit na hapon, habang naglalakad si Juan sa tabi ng kalsada, nakakita siya ng isang puno ng bayabas na puno ng malalaki at hinog na bunga. Gustong-gusto niyang kumain ng bayabas, ngunit napakataas ng puno at ayaw niyang magkapagod sa pag-akyat.

slides.title: Kuwento: Si Juan Tamad - Bahagi 2
slides.text: Sa halip na umakyat o kumuha ng mahabang kawayan upang sungkitin ang bayabas, humiga si Juan sa ilalim ng puno. Tumingala siya at tinitigan ang isang napakahinog na bayabas na nakabitin sa mababang sanga. 'Hihiga na lang ako rito at bubuksan ko ang aking bibig. Hihintayin ko na lang na mahulog ang bayabas sa aking bibig,' sabi ni Juan sa sarili. Nanatili siyang nakahiga sa ilalim ng puno ng ilang oras, habang lumilipas ang araw at nagugutom siya. Ang mga ibon naman ay dumapo sa puno at kinain ang ilang bayabas sa itaas habang pinapanood si Juan.

slides.title: Kuwento: Si Juan Tamad - Bahagi 3
slides.text: Nang dumaan ang kanyang ina sa kalsada at nakita si Juan na nakanganga sa ilalim ng puno, napa-iling ito sa lungkot at galit. 'Juan! Bakit ka nakahiga diyan?' tanong ng ina. 'Ina, hinihintay ko pong mahulog ang bayabas sa aking bibig,' sagot ni Juan. Sinabi ng kanyang ina, 'Napakalaking bayabas niyan, Juan! Kung tumayo ka lamang at kinuha ito gamit ang iyong kamay, kanina ka pa busog! Walang pagkain na kusang mahuhulog sa bibig ng taong ayaw gumawa.' Nahiya si Juan at tumayo upang tulungan ang kanyang ina sa pagdadala ng mga kahoy.

slides.title: Talasalitaan at Pag-aaral ng Salita
slides.text: Mga Bokabularyo: • Binansagan - Binigyan ng palayaw o pangalan batay sa ugali. • Sungkitin - Kumuha ng bagay sa itaas gamit ang stick o kawayan. • Kusang - Nangyayari nang walang tulong o kusa ng iba. Tutok sa Phonics: Magsanay sa pagbigkas ng tunog ng 'j' o 'h' sa 'juan', 'hinog', at 'higa'.

slides.title: Mga Tanong sa Pag-unawa
slides.text: Mga Tanong: 1. Bakit binansagan si Juan na 'Juan Tamad'? 2. Paano nakuha ni Juan ang bayabas ayon sa kanyang plano? 3. Ano ang sinabi ng kanyang ina nang makita siyang nakahiga sa ilalim ng puno?

slides.title: Mabuting Aral at Pagninilay
slides.text: Kaisipan: Ang mga pangarap at pangangailangan ay makakamit lamang sa pamamagitan ng pagkilos. Ang katamaran ay nagdadala ng gutom at kawalan ng pag-unlad. Hindi dapat iasa sa tadhana o pagkakataon ang mga bagay na maaari namang pagtrabahuhan. Talakayin: Ano ang mga layunin mo sa buhay na nangangailangan ng iyong pagsisikap upang maabot?
